run_focus: Day-to-day lab workflows with heavy screenshot scrutiny against the R19-R21 schema. Core vocabulary: **accessions** are biological concepts (tree/genotype/MA line) that carry pipeline status (active / waiting / storage / completed / archived) and a 0-3 star priority; physical instances attach via `of:` as sample / extraction / library / pool / tube. **Chemicals** (EHS-registered, purple pill, fa-atom) are distinct from **reagents** (teal pill) and from **kits** / **enzymes**; bottles are the instance type for all of these. Emphasize: accession lifecycle (create a concept, add instances, change status into `storage`, set stars, swap `people:` members), bottle placement on benches / shelves / cabinets / fridges, grid placement of tubes in freezer boxes, the new 12-tab nav + '+' overflow popover, and the concept/instance orientation banners at the top of Accessions and Inventory. Every action before+after screenshot, every screenshot Read and visually evaluated.

behavior_modifiers.name: rushed
behavior_modifiers.description: Types fast, doesn't wait for saves, clicks away quickly. Tests race conditions and unsaved state.

behavior_modifiers.name: careful
behavior_modifiers.description: Reads every page, verifies rendered output after every save, checks content via GitHub API. Catches stale render bugs.

behavior_modifiers.name: confused
behavior_modifiers.description: Clicks wrong buttons, uses search with typos, tries to save without required fields. Tests error handling.

behavior_modifiers.name: thorough
behavior_modifiers.description: Fills every optional field, uploads multiple images, creates deeply nested content with many wikilinks. Tests limits.

behavior_modifiers.name: mobile
behavior_modifiers.description: Uses 375x812 viewport throughout. Tests responsive layout, touch targets, bottom nav, image upload on mobile.

behavior_modifiers.name: adversarial
behavior_modifiers.description: HTML/script injection, 500+ char strings, emoji-heavy names, Unicode edge cases. Tests input sanitization.

behavior_modifiers.name: impatient
behavior_modifiers.description: Double-clicks save, refreshes mid-save, back-button after submit. Tests interruption handling.

behavior_modifiers.name: explorer
behavior_modifiers.description: URL manipulation (?doc=nonexistent), opens pages in unexpected order, deep-links to deleted items. Tests error states.

behavior_modifiers.name: ui_critic
behavior_modifiers.description: Focuses on visual coherence -- does the pill color match the type? Is the breadcrumb correct? Is the grid cell rendering the right child? Is spacing consistent? Screenshot-heavy.

behavior_modifiers.name: cross_linker
behavior_modifiers.description: Obsessed with links -- follows every wikilink, verifies bidirectional backlinks, checks that object pills render with correct icon+title, tests [[ autocomplete everywhere.

scenario_cards: Ethanol bottle day: Inventory -> Add new bottle of Ethanol Absolute (from the ethanol-absolute concept, which is now a `chemical` with purple pill). Give it a lot number, expiration, assign location (cabinet-flammable). Verify the concept popup column 3 now lists this bottle.

Aliquot day: Take an existing accession (e.g. `accession-pistachio-4` or any accession from /app/accessions.html). Create 6 new tube instances (`of: [[accessions/<slug>]]`, type: tube) named tube-<slug>-aliquot-1..-6, each pointing to a fresh grid cell inside a -80 freezer box. Verify the accession popup lists them as instances-in-lab.

New freezer box day: Create new box 'Library Prep Kit Storage' (grid 10x10) on an existing shelf. Add 3 tubes into it at specific positions. Verify the grid renders them, the breadcrumb (room->freezer->shelf->box->tube) is complete, and the lab-map tree shows the new box as a child of the shelf.
Reorder day: Scan inventory, pick 3 items that are actually low, toggle need-more on each. Verify dashboard needs-ordering count updates. Toggle one back off, verify count decrements.

Lab notebook full day: Create a new notebook entry under your own folder. Add a title, body with 3 section headers, a table of 4 columns x 6 rows, a callout, a wikilink to an accession, and 2 images (annotate one). Save. Reload. Verify everything round-trips.
Print protocol day: Open a protocol with images. Click print. Verify the print preview opens, images load fully (not blank), pagination doesn't clip figures, and no page break lands inside a callout.
Rename + relink day: Rename an existing protocol. Verify any wikilinks pointing to the old slug still work (redirects or auto-updated). Check backlinks on any referring objects still render.

Equipment move day: Pick 3 bottle instances currently on shelf A. Use the locations picker to move all 3 to shelf B. Verify the tree view updates on lab-map, shelf A's children-count decrements, shelf B's increments.

Onboarding day: Open /app/people.html and click 'Join the Lab' / Add Person. Run the onboarding wizard end-to-end — pick protocols, create notebook folder, first entry. Verify the resulting person card + notebook folder exist; then delete to clean up.

Chemical concept+bottle linking: Pick a `chemical` concept (purple pill, EHS-registered, e.g. ethanol-absolute) with one or more bottles. Create 2 new bottle instances (distinct lot numbers). Verify the concept popup column 3 lists all bottles; verify Inventory collapses bottles under the concept with an instance count.

Cross-page wikilink crawl: From the dashboard, follow a link to a protocol. From the protocol, follow a wikilink to a chemical. From the chemical popup to a physical bottle. From the bottle to its location (shelf/cabinet). From the location breadcrumb back to a room. Verify every hop has the right pill color + icon.

Photo-heavy notebook: Upload 5 images to one notebook entry. Annotate 3 (boxes + labels). Resize 2 smaller. Save. Reload. Verify all 5 render at the right size with annotations intact; GitHub API fallback kicks in on private-repo images.

Mobile lab notebook: On 375x812 viewport, create a notebook entry with a photo + text. Verify the Aa mobile toolbar works, the fab button layout, image upload on touch, and that the '+' More popover in the top nav is reachable from the bottom tab bar.

Bulk accession tracker: Open /app/accessions.html. Add 10 accessions in rapid succession with varying status (active, waiting, storage, completed, archived) and varying priority (0-3 stars). Filter by each status in turn. Verify counts on each stat card match filtered row count; verify stars render as the right count in the Priority column.

Search every surface: Global search for an obscure term (e.g. 'DNeasy' or 'MS basal salt'). Verify it returns hits across inventory, protocols, notebooks, and accessions — and that Inventory hits carry the correct pill color per type (chemical=purple, reagent=teal, kit=orange, enzyme=green).

Backlinks sanity: Pick any object with known inbound references (e.g. an accession referenced by tubes, a chemical referenced by bottles). Open its popup. Verify the backlinks pane classifies instances-in-lab correctly (sample/extraction/library/pool/tube/bottle) regardless of whether they link via `of:` or body wikilinks.

Calendar add/delete: Click an empty hour cell in the week grid — the Add Block modal should pre-fill that date + hour. Create the block with [[ autocomplete on Member(s). Verify it renders. Delete it. Verify it disappears.
Waste container: Create a new waste container for ethanol-contaminated gloves. Verify days-held starts at 0 and updates on reload.

Accession status overhaul: Pick an accession currently in `active`. Change status to `storage` (the new 5th bucket, blue pill, inventory_2 icon). Set priority to 3 stars. Update `people:` to add a second member via [[ autocomplete. Add a `status_note`. Save. Reload. Verify every field round-trips and the Accessions tracker row shows the updated pill color + star count.

12-tab nav overflow: On a ~1024px viewport, scan the top nav. Verify the '+' More button is present with at least one primary tab hidden behind it. Click '+' and open the popover; navigate to one of the overflow tabs from there. On mobile viewport (375x812), verify the bottom tab bar shows Wiki / Notebooks / Accessions / Inventory and the '+' popover is reachable for overflow tabs.

Orientation banners: Open /app/accessions.html and screenshot the top — verify the concept/instance orientation banner is present with a purple border and text distinguishing accession concepts from physical instances (sample/extraction/library/pool). Open /app/inventory.html and verify the teal-bordered banner explaining chemical/reagent concepts vs bottles.

Chemical pill recolor: Walk the Inventory page and spot-check 5 chemicals (should be purple #6a1b9a with fa-atom icon) against 5 reagents (should be teal). Open a popup for one of each and verify the same color survives inside the modal header pill.

`of:` picker includes accessions: Open the editor for a new object of type=tube (or sample/extraction/library/pool). Click the `of:` field — the datalist should offer `accessions/...` entries (not just `samples/` or `resources/`). Pick one, save, verify the tube's parent resolves and the accession's backlinks pane lists the new tube as an instance-in-lab.

Star priority sort: On /app/accessions.html, sort by priority. Verify 3-star rows come first, 0-star last. Click a 2-star row, open its popup, edit priority to 3, save, reload, verify it re-sorts to the top.

screenshot_protocol.rule_1: Every click, save, navigation, or form submit MUST have a before AND after screenshot.
screenshot_protocol.rule_2: Save all cycle screenshots under /tmp/qa-screenshots/cycleN/ (create the dir).
screenshot_protocol.rule_3: Name screenshots descriptively: cycleN-stepM-<persona>-<action>.png e.g. cycle9-03-vianney-aliquot-box-popup.png
screenshot_protocol.rule_4: After taking each screenshot, READ it with the Read tool. Do NOT skip. Evaluate: is the UI rendering correctly? Is the right object loaded? Are pills colored right? Are icons right? Is the breadcrumb correct? Is there overflow, clipping, or missing text?
screenshot_protocol.rule_5: Write a visual evaluation string per screenshot into the cycle's workflows_tested entry under screenshots: [{path, what_it_shows, evaluation, issues_found}].
screenshot_protocol.rule_6: If a screenshot reveals a visual issue (even a cosmetic one), log it as a bug with severity: visual. Attempt a fix if in scope (app/ or docs/ only).

screenshots.evaluation: Cabinet popup renders correctly with breadcrumb Robbins Hall 0170 / Flammable Cabinet, Type=Container pill, fields panel, and a scrollable CONTENTS list of 16+ bottles.

screenshots.shows: Verify run of Flammable Cabinet popup after moving a test bottle, CONTENTS includes 'Ethanol Absolute (verify)' entry
screenshots.evaluation: Fix verified. Cabinet contents pane correctly lists the newly-moved bottle immediately after the save flow completes, no deploy required. Hierarchy cache invalidation works.

bugs.summary: Children lists and breadcrumbs rendered from Lab.hierarchy._graph went stale after a save via patchObjectIndex — a new bottle/tube/box did not appear in its parent's CONTENTS until full reload and the child's own popup had no breadcrumb
bugs.evidence: step07 bench popup did not list the new bottle; step08 bottle popup missing breadcrumb; step15 cabinet-after-move did not list the bottle (log flag hasBottle=false)
bugs.root_cause: Lab.hierarchy built a derived cache (children map + parent chain) from the index once and never invalidated it when patchObjectIndex / removeFromObjectIndex mutated the underlying store.

fixes: Invalidate Lab.hierarchy cache from patchObjectIndex / removeFromObjectIndex so children-lists and breadcrumbs reflect newly-saved objects immediately (app/js/github-api.js, commit 3d94ff8)
observations: Concept -> instance relationship works: a new bottle with `of: resources/ethanol-absolute` shows up in the concept's CONTENTS/backlinks immediately via patchLinkIndex from cycle 1.
Parent relationship previously lagged until hierarchy cache was invalidated — fixed in cycle 2.
Move-between-parents (bench -> cabinet) works by saving the file with the new `parent:` frontmatter; both the old and new parents reflect the change after the cache-invalidation fix.
Inventory page renders the new bottle as a row in the ethanol-grouped section; concept grouping hides individual bottles under the concept pill.

screenshots.evaluation: BUG: dashboard did not reflect the 3 toggled items. Counts did not change, alerts list is stale. Cause: dashboard.html init() called raw `fetch(BASE + 'object-index.json')` rather than Lab.gh.fetchObjectIndex, bypassing the localStorage patch overlay.

screenshots.shows: POST-FIX verification — Dashboard Inventory Status now shows 224 In Stock / 2 Needs More / 1 Out of Stock and the alert list includes the 2 toggled items (1,8-Naphthalic anhydride + 1-HYDROXY-3-(3-TRIFLUOROMETHYLPHENYL)UREA, both with amber Needs More pills) plus the pre-existing Native Barcoding Kit (red Out of Stock)
screenshots.evaluation: FIX CONFIRMED. Dashboard now applies the localStorage patch overlay via Lab.gh.fetchObjectIndex. Counts match expected values. Alert list correctly includes newly-toggled items with correct amber/red badges and material icons (warning_amber triangle, cancel X). Recent Updates column on the right shows the cycle-3 cleanup commits. Layout and typography consistent with the rest of the dashboard.

bugs.summary: Dashboard Inventory Status card showed stale counts and alert list after inventory items had their status toggled on the inventory page
bugs.evidence: step09-after-dashboard-after-3-toggles.png is identical to step01 baseline despite 3 items being toggled to needs_more; programmatic check returned picksFound:[false,false,false] and alertCount unchanged
bugs.root_cause: dashboard.html init() called `fetch(BASE + 'object-index.json')` directly instead of going through Lab.gh.fetchObjectIndex, which applies the localStorage patch overlay populated by patchObjectIndex. The static object-index.json is only rebuilt on deploy, so between an inventory edit and the next deploy the dashboard rendered stale data.

fixes: dashboard.html init() now prefers Lab.gh.fetchObjectIndex (with its localStorage overlay) and falls back to raw fetch only if Lab.gh is unavailable (commit 763ad51)
observations: cycleStatus correctly walks the STATUS_CYCLE ring (in_stock → needs_more → out_of_stock → in_stock) and commits each step to GitHub.
Inventory page stat cards (In Stock / Needs More / Out of Stock) are immediately correct after each toggle — they read from the in-memory inventory array updated by cycleStatus.
Alert rows on the inventory table get a 4px amber/red left border and a light tint — consistent with the status pill color.
The Inventory Status card on the dashboard is fed from the same object-index, so once the overlay was applied the alert list auto-populated with no other changes.

Dashboard top-stat 'INVENTORY 480' is the raw total of *all* stock+resource objects (including externals), whereas the status counts (224/2/1) only sum tracked statuses — so they don't add up to 480. That is intentional but could confuse a lab member.
Verification file: tests/qa-cycle-3-verify.mjs toggles 2 items, navigates to dashboard, asserts picksFound:[true,true] — kept in the repo as a regression harness for the fix.

bugs.root_cause: renderFields() in app/js/editor-modal.js had a special case for `field.key === 'parent'` that emitted a <span data-parent-pill> placeholder for upgradeParentField() to replace with a proper pill. No equivalent case existed for `field.key === 'of'`, so it fell through to the generic text renderer.

fixes: Added an 'of' field branch in renderFields that emits a <span data-of-pill> placeholder parallel to the parent-pill placeholder, and extended upgradeParentField to also upgrade `[data-of-pill]` placeholders (app/js/editor-modal.js, commit b0da836)

Lab-map filter uses case-insensitive substring match; 'ethanol' matches 'methanol' and '2-mercaptoethanol'.

bugs.root_cause: patchObjectIndex in app/js/github-api.js whitelists fields against INDEX_KEYS. That list did not include the waste-container fields (contents, physical_state, container, hazard_class, started, waste_tag) or the sample-tracker fields (sample_id, species, lead, sequencing_type) even though the server-side scripts/build-object-index.py has them in EXTRACT_KEYS. Every save via patchObjectIndex therefore dropped these fields from the localStorage overlay, so pages that read them rendered blanks during the window between save and next deploy.

fixes: Added waste-container fields (contents, physical_state, container, hazard_class, started, waste_tag) and sample-tracker fields (sample_id, species, lead, sequencing_type) to INDEX_KEYS in app/js/github-api.js so the client list mirrors EXTRACT_KEYS in scripts/build-object-index.py (commit 5c01bed).

Status in the popup field column renders as raw slug 'in_accumulation'; the waste table uses 'In Accumulation' with an amber pill. Minor asymmetry worth a future fix.
Waste containers use `location:` as a free-text string rather than a `parent:` slug, so they do NOT appear in the lab-map hierarchy tree. They're a separate surface on waste.html.
Wiki viewer 404s for newly-created files until the next deploy even when the user is authenticated. Could fall back to Lab.gh.fetchFile for a live read from GitHub.
tests/qa-cycle-5-verify.mjs kept as a regression harness: asserts contents / state='Solid' / days='0 d' / filter + search / reload all survive a freshly-created waste container.

bugs.summary: Sidebar folder count regresses after reload — VIANNEY AHN goes 6→7 on creation but back to 6 after reload because fetchTree returns GitHub's git/trees API view which lags the just-committed file by a few seconds
bugs.evidence: screenshots/cycle6/14-after-reload-rendered.png and 18-sidebar-with-new-entry.png both show count 6 even though we're actively viewing the just-created entry. fetchTree was called fresh on reload and returned a stale list.
bugs.root_cause: fetchTree() in app/js/github-api.js read GitHub's git/trees recursive endpoint with no overlay layer. saveFile and deleteFile did not record their changes anywhere fetchTree could see, so any caller (notebooks.html sidebar, wiki.html, protocols.html) would render a stale tree until the API caught up.

bugs.summary: Notebook entries saved via notebooks.html did not appear in the link-index overlay — wikilinks pointing to sample-pistachio-4, qiagen-dneasy-extraction, etc. did not surface as backlinks on those targets until the next deploy
bugs.evidence: Initial cycle 6 script output 'backlink index hits: {}' — fetchLinkIndex returned no entries from the new doc to its known wikilink targets. notebooks.html saveDoc only called Lab.gh.saveFile, never patchObjectIndex or patchLinkIndex.
bugs.root_cause: The cycle-1 patchLinkIndex fix was wired into editor-modal.js's save flow but notebooks.html's saveDoc / createDoc / deleteDoc did not call any of patchObjectIndex / patchLinkIndex / removeFrom*, so notebook saves bypassed the overlay machinery that pages like editor-modal popups depend on.

fixes: fetchTree localStorage overlay (TREE_PATCH_KEY, 5-min TTL) — saveFile and deleteFile now record their path changes; fetchTree merges them into the API response so any caller (notebooks/wiki/protocols sidebars) sees the just-saved file immediately (app/js/github-api.js, commit e0a0f44)

notebooks.html saveDoc / createDoc / deleteDoc now apply patchObjectIndex + patchLinkIndex (and removeFrom* on delete) so the new entry shows up in the hierarchy + backlink panels of every wikilink target without waiting for a deploy (app/notebooks.html, commit e0a0f44)
observations: Notebooks Add Entry modal works cleanly — the field label adapts ('FILENAME / TITLE' vs 'TITLE') based on the entry type dropdown.
Editor's Insert/Media pill rows are a discoverable way to insert typed pills + media without typing markdown manually.
All rich elements (table, callout, task list, wikilink pills, images) round-trip correctly through save/reload — image fallback via data URLs works pre-deploy.
Wikilinks render as type-colored pills with correct icons even when nested in task items — `[[sample-pistachio-4]]` inside a `- [ ]` becomes a clickable purple Sample pill.
Cross-surface wikilink click from notebook to protocol page works end-to-end via the obj:// URI scheme.
Toast notifications linger ~10s before fading — could be tightened to ~3s (one of the 'editor-opened' screenshots still shows the 'Entry created' toast from several actions earlier).
tests/qa-cycle-6-verify.mjs kept as a regression harness — creates a notebook entry, reloads, asserts fetchTree.sawIt and link-index hit counts > 0 for both wikilink targets.

bugs.summary: BREADCRUMB-DANGLING-SLASH — popup breadcrumb wrapped to two lines and left a standalone '/' separator at the end of line 1 with no following pill, looking like a dangling trailing slash. Visible on any popup with 4+ ancestor levels.
bugs.evidence: cycle1/step06-empty-9x9-box-popup.png, cycle7/08-empty-10x10-box-popup.png, cycle7-reshoot/01-box-popup-with-3-tubes.png — line 1 ends with 'Shelf 1 /' and the next pill ('Library Prep Kit Storage') wraps to line 2 alone
bugs.root_cause: In app/js/hierarchy.js breadcrumbHTML, each crumb pill and each '/' separator was rendered as an independent flex child of a flex-wrap container, so when the total width exceeded the container any flex item (including a bare separator) could be the last item on a wrapped line.

fixes: breadcrumbHTML now groups each separator with its following pill inside an inline-flex span so separator+pill wrap together as one atomic unit. The outer flex still wraps, but can only break between groups, never inside a group — so a '/' can no longer end up orphaned at a wrapped line boundary (app/js/hierarchy.js, commit 25c4999). Verified across 3 viewport widths (1440 / 900 / 520) via tests/qa-cycle-7-verify.mjs; endsWithOrphanSlash:false for all lines at all widths.
observations: 10x10 box grid renders 100 cells cleanly with A-J row labels and 1-10 column labels; occupied cells at all four boundary positions (A1 top-left, C5 middle, J10 bottom-right) render correctly — no edge-case rendering issues for corner cells.
The cycle-2 hierarchy cache invalidation is still holding — a new box shows up as a child of its parent shelf immediately on reopen, and the shelf's tree item-count increments without a redeploy.

Type system is consistent across 5-level chains: dark Room → light-blue Freezer → grey-blue Shelf → brown Box → teal Tube. Colors, icons, and pill shapes match across breadcrumb, fields column, parent pill, and contents list.

Top-nav NAV-TRUNCATION issue from earlier cycles is NOT reproducible at 1440px on the current deploy — all tab labels including 'Lab Map' fit fully. Only the far-right 'Apps' tab gets clipped (shows just 'A'). Either the menu was tightened since cycle 4 or the layout breakpoint changed.

Observed a minor NEW issue: the wikilink '[[dna-quantification-qubit]]' in the box popup body renders as a plain teal pill without a type icon, unlike sibling links to QIAGEN DNeasy Plant Extraction that get the purple book icon. Likely the target slug doesn't resolve in the object index — possibly a typo or an out-of-date link.

bugs.summary: OF-PILL-TRUNCATED-NO-ELLIPSIS — the Of and Parent field pills in the bottle/tube popup's fields column clipped long object titles mid-word. Pill anchor is inline-flex with white-space:nowrap and no max-width; the wrapping span lacked min-width:0 + flex:1 so the pill overflowed past the 300px field column boundary with a hard clip instead of an ellipsis.
bugs.evidence: cycle8/step07-after-click-bottleA-from-concept.png and cycle8/step15-after-open-bottleA-direct.png show the Of pill rendering 'LabBot Freezer Item mnthp' with a hard clip where the full title 'LabBot Freezer Item mnthp11v' would continue. Body pill on same popup renders the full title correctly. Parent pill would have the same issue for long location names.
bugs.root_cause: In app/js/editor-modal.js renderFields() the parent/of placeholder spans did not set min-width:0 or flex:1 on the value span, and upgradeParentField() built the final anchor with the raw pillStyle (white-space:nowrap) without any max-width or inner ellipsis span. With no shrink constraints the flex child grew beyond the ~186px available and overflowed the 300px column.

fixes: Added min-width:0 + flex:1 1 auto + overflow:hidden on the parent/of placeholder spans in renderFields(), set max-width:100%+min-width:0 on the upgraded pill anchor, and wrapped the title text in a child span with overflow:hidden + text-overflow:ellipsis + white-space:nowrap so the icon stays visible while only the title shrinks. Added title="" attribute on the pill for full-title hover tooltip (app/js/editor-modal.js, commit 0088518). Verified at 1440 and 520 viewport widths via tests/qa-cycle-8-verify.mjs — pillInsideCol:true, textEllipsis:true, titleAttr set.
observations: Concept+instance scenario works end-to-end: create 2 bottles with `of:` → concept popup immediately shows them in its CONTENTS column with subtitle disambiguators (quantity + location) — no deploy needed.
Inventory grouping aggregates correctly: a single concept row reports '750 mL (2 bottles)' combining the two new bottle instances, individual bottles hidden under the concept row as intended.

Chemical Cabinet (cabinet-chemical) is a placeholder container auto-created by migrate-containers-to-bottles.py (R5) — 107 children at baseline, 109 after our 2 bottles. Many reagent CONCEPTS live under it (not just physical bottles) because resource cards were auto-assigned parent=cabinet-chemical during migration. Worth a future pass to distinguish concept placement from physical location.
15 reagent concepts have 0 bottle instances out of ~170 reagents; most were picked up by auto-creation of matching bottles from the EHS export.

bugs.summary: CALENDAR-NO-INDEX-PATCH — newly-created calendar events disappeared from the calendar grid after a page reload until the static object-index.json was rebuilt on deploy (~30-60s). Member wikilinks on events also missed being registered as backlinks on their targets during the same window.
bugs.evidence: cycle9/step16-after-reload.png shows the Wed 2pm cell empty even though the just-created QA9 event was visible in step07-after-submit.png and GH API confirmed the file saved. afterReload.landed=false. Same reproducer for weekly recurring series — all 4 occurrences disappear after reload.
bugs.root_cause: calendar.html's openAddEvent() called gh.saveFile() then schedule.push() + renderCalendar(), but never called gh.patchObjectIndex() (which writes to lab_index_patches localStorage) or gh.patchLinkIndex() (lab_link_index_patches). loadSchedule() on reload goes through Lab.gh.fetchObjectIndex which applies the localStorage overlay, but with no patches written there was nothing to merge — so the event fell off the grid until the static JSON rebuilt. Parallel issue: recurrence_id was in scripts/build-object-index.py's EXTRACT_KEYS but missing from app/js/github-api.js's INDEX_KEYS, so even with patchObjectIndex a patched recurring event would lose its series linkage during the overlay window (sibling of cycle-5's WASTE-FIELDS-NOT-IN-INDEX-KEYS).

fixes: openAddEvent in app/calendar.html now calls gh.patchObjectIndex with type=event + title/date/start_time/end_time/member/recurrence_id/created_by AND gh.patchLinkIndex against member+notes after each saveFile so the event appears on the next reload and its member/notes wikilinks register as backlinks on their targets. The in-memory schedule.push also records recurrenceId for just-created recurring events so deleteSeries works without a reload (commit 8a679ba).

INDEX_KEYS in app/js/github-api.js gains 'recurrence_id' to mirror EXTRACT_KEYS in scripts/build-object-index.py — keeps overlay-preserved event entries consistent with server-rebuilt ones (same commit).
Post-fix verify at tests/qa-cycle-9-verify.mjs — creates an event, reloads, asserts block is visible + overlays contain our keys with the right wikilink targets. Kept as a regression harness.

Concept-type guard in hierarchy.js build(): reagent/chemical/buffer/solution/consumable/kit/enzyme/primer objects now ignore their (usually legacy) parent: field, so concepts no longer pollute a container's Contents list and no longer render a physical breadcrumb — closes cycle-8 CONCEPT-HAS-PARENT-BREADCRUMB (commit a92e314). Also removed the two remaining stale LabBot concept fixtures (labbot-freezer-item-mnthp11v, tube-item) in commit f7cfe1f.
observations: Calendar click-to-create flow is solid: click a cell → modal prefills date + start-time from the cell coords + end-time defaults to +1hr → Submit → event lands in the correct grid cell.
layoutBlocks correctly assigns overlapping events to parallel flex columns within the same time range so they don't visually clip each other.

show_when conditional fields (Repeat Until appears only when Repeat=daily/weekly) work cleanly; unchecked hidden fields return '' in the OK handler so stale defaults never leak into the frontmatter — the issue #107 hardening from before is still working.
Weekly recurrence expansion generates N separate files with a shared recurrence_id so deleteSeries can later remove all occurrences in one click — confirmed visually across 2 weeks (Apr 21 + Apr 28).
Mobile 375px viewport is well-handled: day-tabs row appears, grid collapses to single-day view, bottom nav tabs replace top nav. No overflow. Works with the FAB.

Known carryover at 1440px: 'Wiki' tab in the top nav clips to 'Wik' — the cycle-8 TOPNAV-LAST-ITEM-CLIPPED fix tightened padding but 'Wiki' still loses its final character. Worth another 1-2px trim or a slightly wider breakpoint.

Member(s) field on event popups shows the raw [[wikilink]] syntax instead of rendering as a person pill — symmetric with cycle-4's OF-FIELD-NOT-CLICKABLE bug pattern. renderFields in editor-modal.js has special cases for parent/of; would need a parallel branch for member to complete it.

Member color assignment is alphabetical by index, so adding a new member rotates existing members' colors (all barb-m blocks went green→blue when Dr. Monroe joined). A stable hash-of-slug color would avoid that.

tests/qa-cycle-9.mjs + tests/qa-cycle-9-verify.mjs kept as harnesses: the main script stresses click-to-create, editor popup, filter, delete, reload, mobile, recurring weekly series, cleanup; the verify script asserts the overlay-patch fix persists a just-created event across reload and captures both patch keys.
All 5 cycle-9 test event files (1 single + 4 recurring + 1 verify) cleaned up via gh API DELETE before commit.

fixes: app/js/nav.js: tightened desktop tab padding to 12px 8px (gap 4px) and narrow-desktop (769-1280px) to 12px 6px (gap 3px) so all 12 tabs including 'Wiki' fit at 1440 (commit d8c96cc)

app/calendar.html: replaced alphabetical-index MEMBER_COLORS lookup with a djb2 hash (hashStringToInt) modulo MEMBER_COLORS.length so each member name maps to a stable color regardless of who else is in the schedule (commit d8c96cc)

bugs.summary: DOUBLE-FRONTMATTER-BAR — parent's frontmatter bar stays visible above the print-preview iframe while the iframe also embeds its own copy, so the user sees two 'Protocol' pills
bugs.evidence: step08, step12, step13, step16, step20 all show the duplicate (one in the grey margin top-left, one inside the iframe page header)
bugs.root_cause: togglePrintPreview hides #renderedDoc but does not hide its sibling .frontmatter-bar; showPrintPreview deliberately copies the parent's .frontmatter-bar HTML into the iframe content so it gets paginated with the document — but the parent's copy is never hidden, leaving both visible

bugs.summary: FAB-NOT-HIDDEN-IN-PRINT — Print FAB and Issue Reporter FAB are not in the parent's @media print rule, so Cmd+P from the parent prints them on every page
bugs.evidence: step13 (print media emulated) shows both FABs still visible at bottom-right
bugs.root_cause: protocols.html @media print { display:none } list covered nav/sidebar/hamburger/edit-bar/password-gate but missed #print-preview-fab and #issue-reporter-btn

bugs.summary: PROTOCOL-IMAGES-BROKEN — pipette-school.md and gel-electrophoresis.md reference Wikimedia upload.wikimedia.org thumbs that the live site fails to load (HTTP 429 from Varnish), so both the rendered protocol and the print preview show broken-image icons + alt text where the figures should be
bugs.evidence: step05 shows the broken image icon for Micropipettes-on-stand on the live protocol view; step10 shows the same broken state inside the print-preview iframe; curl with a Chrome UA returned 429 from upload.wikimedia.org for the same thumb URL
bugs.root_cause: Hotlinking Wikimedia thumbnails without consistent UA + Referer is rate-limited by Varnish — works intermittently for end users in browsers but fails in QA + sometimes for real users. Local replacements in docs/images/ are 1-2KB stub placeholders, not real images, so swapping URLs would currently make the situation worse.

fixes: app/protocols.html: extended the parent's @media print rule to also hide #print-preview-fab + #issue-reporter-btn + .proto-content > .frontmatter-bar (the iframe embeds its own copy), and added a body.print-preview-active rule that hides .proto-content > .frontmatter-bar so the print preview overlay no longer shows two 'Protocol' pills (commit 1 of cycle 11)
observations: Print preview pipeline produces correct page counts (pipette=6, gel=5, autoclave=4), correct page-number footers, correct flexible 1056px page heights, no orphaned headings (orphans=[]), and zero raw [[wikilink]] leaks (all upgrade to outlined pills inside the iframe).

Image-load gate before pagination uses a 4-second per-image timeout with addEventListener('load'/'error') + setTimeout(finish) — robust, but pagination still proceeds if Wikimedia 429s; broken images render at 0 height which is OK for paper but visually distracting on screen.

PROTOCOL-IMAGES-BROKEN is left OPEN this cycle. Local docs/images/ stubs are 1.9KB placeholders, not viable swap-ins. A real fix needs Grey or a lab member to either populate those local files with real photos OR the protocols to switch to lab-owned image assets.

Print FAB click handler correctly targets iframe.contentWindow.print() — printing only the iframe contents, which is the right behavior (parent FABs would not print regardless). The new @media print hides cover the Cmd+P-from-parent edge case.
No artifacts created on GitHub this cycle — print preview is a read-only flow, nothing to clean up.

Post-deploy verify (qa-cycle-11-verify.mjs) confirmed both fixes live: parent .frontmatter-bar is display:none in print preview (FIX1), #print-preview-fab + #issue-reporter-btn are display:none under @media print (FIX2), and the parent fm bar restores to display:flex after Exit Preview (no regression on normal view).

PROTOCOL-IMAGES-BROKEN (carry-over, OPEN): Wikimedia upload.wikimedia.org thumbs in pipette-school + gel-electrophoresis return HTTP 429. Local replacements in docs/images/ are 1.9KB stub placeholders, not real images, so a URL swap would currently make the live state worse. Left open as a non-Claude task — needs Grey or a lab member to populate the local stubs with real photos OR find a reliably-served CDN for the educational images. No app-side fix is appropriate here.

Verified rendered wikilinks are typed object pills on both popups and the wiki page. Inspected inventory table rows for ethanol filter.

bugs: INVENTORY-LOCATION-RAW-SLUG (visual, fixed in cycle 13): Inventory table LOCATION column rendered raw slugs like 'locations/cabinet-flammable' inside plain grey badges. Every other object surface (popups, breadcrumbs, body wikilinks, lab-map tree) resolves the same slug to a typed pill with the resolved title and icon. Fix: loadInventory builds a locationsBySlug lookup from the object-index; new locationCell(loc) helper renders the cell as a typed object pill (Lab.types.pillStyle + pillContent) when the slug resolves, falls back to a plain grey badge for free-text, multi-location aggregates ('N locations'), or unresolved slugs. Applied to both the desktop table and the mobile card view.

INSTANCE-TITLES-AMBIGUOUS (minor, logged, not fixed): On a concept popup, the CONTENTS->instances list shows each bottle as title+subtitle (parent). When N bottles share a concept AND live at the same parent (e.g. two Ethanol 70% bottles both on 'bench'), the rows are visually indistinguishable. A better disambiguator would be lot number, created date, or the bottle's slug tail. Low priority — most real cases differ by parent.
fixes: INVENTORY-LOCATION-RAW-SLUG → app/inventory.html: new locationCell(loc) + locationsBySlug; table + mobile card use typed pill when slug resolves
observations: Scenario: PI Friday link-integrity audit. 21 on-site screenshots + 1 verification screenshot.
All 4 backlink-heavy popups rendered correctly: ethanol-70 (24 refs), gel-electrophoresis (16 refs), dna-ladder-1kb-plus (1 ref), Pistachio Leaf 1 (0 refs).
Bidirectional wikilinks confirmed end-to-end: gel-electrophoresis's backlinks include dna-ladder-1kb-plus, and dna-ladder's body renders [[gel-electrophoresis]] as a clickable purple protocol pill.
Breadcrumb chains render correctly up to 5 levels (room / freezer / shelf / box / tube), wrapping to 2 lines as needed.
Freezer box grid rendered both occupied (teal A1/A2) and empty cells with the right labels. Grid click-through opens tube popup with full breadcrumb.
Lab-map filter for 'pistachio' matched deeply nested descendants and preserved hierarchy — excellent UX.
Seed-sterilization wiki page renders [[ethanol-70]] as class='object-pill' with teal background rgba(0,150,136,0.07). Consistent with popup pills.

Inventory search 'ethanol' returned 5 concept rows (2-Mercaptoethanol, Ethanol 70%, Ethanol Absolute, Formaldehyde PFA, Methanol); bottles were correctly rolled up under Ethanol 70% row ('2 bottles') — no bottle leaks to the top-level list.

During this run, an abandoned earlier cycle-13 attempt (rename+relink + bulk-sample-tracker hybrid) left cleanup commits and a stale /tmp/qa-cycle-13.mjs script — that run never made it into state. Screenshots for this cycle were saved under /tmp/qa-screenshots/cycle13b/ to avoid collision.

bugs: RENAME-DOUBLE-CLICK-FAILURE-TOAST (functional/ux, fixed in cycle 15, commit c3fb6b6): double-clicking the Rename button fires a spurious 'Rename failed: Not Found' red toast even though the first click already succeeded in renaming the file. Cause: no re-entry guard in renameDoc — both clicks synchronously entered the function before the first `await Lab.modal.prompt`, and the second call's fetchFile on the old path 404s. Fix: a renameInFlight flag set BEFORE the first await and cleared in a finally block, so the synchronous second click returns at the guard.

DOUBLE-LOAD-ERROR-PREFIX (cosmetic/ux, fixed in cycle 15, commit c3fb6b6): loadDoc error UI in protocols.html / wiki.html / projects.html / notebooks.html rendered 'Failed to load: Failed to load docs/<path> (HTTP 404)' — the UI wrapper prefix duplicated the thrown error's prefix, and the raw file path + HTTP status leaked out. Fix: added Lab.formatLoadError(err) helper in shared.js that strips the redundant 'Failed to load ' prefix and maps any 'HTTP 404'/'(404)' to a single friendly line: 'Document not found. It may have been renamed or deleted.'. Applied across all four load-error renderings.

SEARCH-PROTOCOL-PLACEHOLDER-CLIPPED (cosmetic, fixed in cycle 15, commit c3fb6b6): protocols sidebar search input placeholder 'Search protocols...' clipped to 'Search protocol' because the input width is constrained by two adjacent icon buttons. Shortened to 'Search...' matching the cycle-8 notebooks fix.
fixes: app/protocols.html renameDoc: renameInFlight guard set before the first await and released in a finally block — double/triple-click Rename is now idempotent, no spurious failure toast (commit c3fb6b6)

app/js/shared.js: new Lab.formatLoadError(err) helper that maps 404 responses to 'Document not found. It may have been renamed or deleted.' and strips the redundant 'Failed to load ' prefix from other errors (commit c3fb6b6)

app/protocols.html + app/wiki.html + app/projects.html + app/notebooks.html: loadDoc error UI now uses Lab.formatLoadError so the user sees a clean single-line message instead of 'Failed to load: Failed to load docs/foo.md (HTTP 404)' (commit c3fb6b6)
app/protocols.html: sidebar search placeholder 'Search protocols...' → 'Search...' (commit c3fb6b6)
observations: Rename + relink scenario validated end-to-end: create protocol, create notebook with both [[slug]] + [[dir/slug]] wikilinks, rename protocol, reload notebook, both pills rewrite to new slug and render with the right type color + icon. The QA13 updateWikilinksAfterRename plumbing is doing its job; this cycle confirms it in a formal run and logs it.
Impatient double-click was the star bug of this cycle — silent success but visible error toast is the worst kind of UI confusion. Fixed with a simple re-entry flag.
The 404 error UX improvement applies broadly: any deep-link to a deleted/renamed/moved file now says 'Document not found' instead of exposing the internal docs/.../foo.md path and a raw HTTP status.

Two leftover LabBot Test Protocol fixtures (mnthp11v, mntzkf4h in Wet Lab Basics) visible in protocol sidebar; cycle-9 stale-LabBot-fixture cleanup covered the reagent concepts but not these protocol stubs. Logged as observation — deleting them should be a small vault housekeeping sweep.
All 3 test files cleaned up via gh API DELETE at end of cycle.
tests/qa-cycle-15.mjs kept as regression harness; verify script run against the deployed fixes confirmed rename_failed_toast_present:false, friendly_err:true, placeholder_clean:true.

Bulk-selects all 10 QA17 rows via the header checkbox (filter search=QA17), bulk-deletes, and verifies zero left on both UI and GitHub. All functional deltas matched expectations; initial Total=228 and final Total=228 confirm clean cleanup.

screenshots.evaluation: FUNCTIONAL BUG: stats and data correctly updated, but bulk-action bar remained visible with stale '10 selected' text even after selectedIds.clear(). Root cause: renderTable() early-returns when the filtered items list is empty, skipping the updateBulkBar() call, so the bar keeps its last-rendered state. Fixed this cycle by calling updateBulkBar() in both the empty and non-empty branches of renderTable.
issues_found: BULK-BAR-STALE-AFTER-EMPTY-RENDER (visual/ux, fixed this cycle): After bulk-delete of all filtered rows, the bulk action bar stayed visible with stale '10 selected' text and the previously-chosen status dropdown. Root cause: renderTable's `if (items.length === 0) { ...; return }` early return skipped the updateBulkBar() call. Fix: invoke updateBulkBar() in the empty branch too so the bar reflects the now-empty selectedIds Set.

Root cause: renderPersonDashboard only aggregates INBOUND backlinks (items wikilinking TO people/<slug>); the wizard creates OUTBOUND edges from the person. bugs.summary: PEOPLE-CARD-PILLS-BARE-SLUG — the connected pills on every person card pointed to `?doc=<bare-slug>` on protocols.html/projects.html, but loadDoc fetches `docs/<docPath>.md`. For any protocol living in a subfolder (wet-lab/autoclave, library-prep/dneasy, etc.) the bare slug 404s. Affected every card that had a connected pill, not just newly-onboarded ones.
bugs.evidence: cycle18/17-protocol-with-new-person-backlink.png: clicking autoclave pill landed on 'Document not found. It may have been renamed or deleted.' at protocols.html?doc=autoclave. Every person's connected pills had the same issue.
bugs.root_cause: loadPeople() pill renderer in app/people.html line 271 used `'?doc=' + esc(wl.slug)` — the bare slug extracted from the wikilink. Should have used the resolved object's full path (obj.path minus `.md`, minus `/index` for folder-style pages).

bugs.summary: PERSON-DASHBOARD-IGNORES-FORWARD-LINKS — the onboarding wizard writes `[[protocol]]` / `[[project]]` wikilinks into the person's body (as declarations of 'I work on these'), but renderPersonDashboard only aggregates INBOUND backlinks (items wikilinking TO people/<slug>). Result: newly onboarded users see a 0-across-the-board dashboard despite having picked protocols and projects.
bugs.evidence: cycle18/12-person-dashboard-top.png: 0 Notebooks / 0 Protocols / 0 Events / 0 Inventory / 0 Locations / 0 Projects with 'No connected items yet' empty state, even though the person's own body had 4 wikilinks.
bugs.root_cause: renderPersonDashboard in app/people.html only looked at edge.target === 'people/<slug>' to build backlinkSources. Outbound edges (where source === 'people/<slug>') were ignored. The wizard creates outbound links only.

bugs.summary: NOTEBOOK-ENTRY-NOT-PATCHED — the onboarding wizard creates a first notebook entry at docs/notebooks/<slug>/<today>.md but only patchObjectIndex'd the person card, not the notebook entry. The dashboard's Lab Notebooks filter (path startsWith notebooks/<slug>/) therefore missed the new entry until the next deploy.
bugs.evidence: cycle18/12-person-dashboard-top.png Notebooks stat=0 despite a notebook entry being visible at notebooks.html (cycle18/14-notebooks-baseline.png).
bugs.root_cause: createPersonFiles in app/people.html called patchObjectIndex only for the personPath, not for the entry path.

fixes: app/people.html loadPeople() pill renderer — when wl.slug resolves against objectLookup, build the href from `obj.path.replace(/\.md$/, '').replace(/\/index$/, '')` (stripping /index for folder-style project pages) instead of the bare wl.slug. Falls back to wl.slug only when the lookup fails. Verified: clicking the autoclave pill at /app/people.html now navigates to protocols.html?doc=wet-lab%2Fautoclave and loads the file correctly (cycle18-verify/02).

app/people.html renderPersonDashboard — in addition to the inbound backlinkSources already gathered, collect forwardSlugs from (a) link-index edges where source === 'people/<slug>' and (b) a fallback body parse of the person file for [[wikilinks]]. Resolve each forward slug against the object-index (slug OR relPath key), then merge the resulting items into backlinkedItems (de-duped). Sections now populate whether the relationship is declared inbound or outbound.

app/people.html createPersonFiles — after patchObjectIndex for the personPath, also patchObjectIndex the entryPath with {type:'notebook', title:'<date> — <name>'} so renderPersonDashboard's Lab Notebooks filter finds the new entry immediately. (commit 52fcfd3)
observations: Onboarding wizard 4-step flow works end-to-end; auto-creation succeeds in ~3s across 3 GitHub API writes (person md + gitkeep + first notebook entry).
Person card Connected pills visually represent the person's declared work (protocols + projects) — a useful at-a-glance view of 'who does what'.
Post-fix, a freshly-onboarded user lands on a dashboard that already shows their protocols + projects + first notebook entry. Removes the previous 'empty-feeling first day' UX.

Minor: wizard's 'Write your first lab notebook entry' checklist link targets notebooks.html?person=<slug>, but notebooks.html ignores that query param. Out of scope this cycle; logged as NOTEBOOKS-PERSON-PARAM-IGNORED (minor ux, open).

Minor: issue-reporter FAB bottom-right sits visually close to the wizard's Next button at 375px mobile viewport. Could hide the FAB while the wizard overlay is open. Logged as FAB-NEAR-WIZARD-NEXT-MOBILE (cosmetic, open).

Added 'people' + 'profile' to routes_tested. Both routes now exercised: /app/people.html (grid + dashboard + wizard), /app/profile.html?user=<slug> (empty state for new users). /app/graph.html still untested.
tests/qa-cycle-18.mjs and tests/qa-cycle-18-verify.mjs kept as harnesses. Verify asserts pill hrefs contain 'wet-lab/' and fresh-user dashboard counts are 1 Notebook / 2 Protocols / 1 Project.
All cycle-18 test artifacts (1 cycle person + entry + gitkeep, 1 verify person + entry + gitkeep) cleaned up via gh API DELETE.

' whereas inventory/waste use the styled .empty-state class with the search_off icon. Swapped to the standard pattern.

Also minor UX note: Recent Entries on the right pane is independent of the sidebar search, which may confuse a user who types into the search expecting global filtering. Log as observation. Fixed the cosmetic inconsistency this cycle; deferred the right-pane-filtering question.
issues_found: NOTEBOOKS-EMPTY-STATE-MINIMAL (cosmetic, fixed this cycle): swapped plain-text empty state for the styled .empty-state div with search_off icon, matching inventory/protocols.

NOTEBOOKS-RECENT-ENTRIES-NOT-FILTERED (ux, logged, NOT fixed this cycle): sidebar search filters the folder tree but leaves the right-pane Recent Entries list unchanged, creating a mismatch when the sidebar shows 'No notebooks match' while the right pane lists 10+ entries. Debatable — Recent Entries is globally recent by design, so changing it may be wrong. Leaving as an observation, not a bug.

screenshots.shows: Wiki sidebar shows 'No documents match' text; the main pane knowledge graph still displays all ~300 nodes at full intensity, unfiltered.
screenshots.evaluation: BUG: wiki search is sidebar-only. The main knowledge graph does not dim, fade, or filter nodes when the sidebar search matches 0 docs. A confused user would see 'No documents match' yet still see a full graph and wonder what's going on. Deferred this cycle — graph filtering is a larger change.
issues_found: WIKI-GRAPH-NOT-FILTERED (ux, NOT fixed this cycle): wiki.html sidebar search filters the folder tree but leaves the knowledge graph in the main pane completely unchanged, even when sidebar says 'No documents match'. Graph should either be filtered or non-matching nodes dimmed. Larger change — logged for a follow-up cycle.

screenshots.evaluation: Waste already has the styled empty state — matches inventory. Confirms protocols + notebooks were the outliers.

Fixed this cycle by toggling body.wizard-open and hiding #issue-reporter-btn via CSS.
issues_found: FAB-OVERLAPS-WIZARD-NEXT-MOBILE (visual, fixed this cycle): issue-reporter FAB overlaps the wizard Next button at 375px, not just 'sits near' it as cycle-18 described. Fix adds body.wizard-open toggle to hide the FAB while the overlay is visible.

issues_found: NOTEBOOKS-PERSON-PARAM-IGNORED (carry-over from cycle 18, fixed this cycle)

bugs.summary: FAB-OVERLAPS-WIZARD-NEXT-MOBILE — at 375px mobile viewport, #issue-reporter-btn (bottom-right, 44×44, z-index 10001) overlapped the wizard's Next button by ~34px horizontally. Cycle 18 flagged this as 'FAB near wizard Next' but the geometry measurement in cycle 19 shows actual overlap, not just proximity.
bugs.evidence: cycle19/step26-people-wizard-open-mobile.png — two blue shapes, Next button partially hidden behind FAB. Bounding rects: FAB {x:313, right:357, y:688, bottom:732}, Next {left:236, right:347, top:663, bottom:701}. overlap:true.
bugs.root_cause: No z-index or visibility interaction between wizard overlay and the fixed-positioned issue-reporter FAB. The FAB sits at fixed bottom-right across the whole app and does not listen for any app-specific modal state.

bugs.summary: NOTEBOOKS-PERSON-PARAM-IGNORED — ?person=<slug> URL param had no effect on notebooks.html sidebar. A newly-onboarded user clicking 'Write your first lab notebook entry' from the wizard checklist landed on the full unfiltered list instead of their own folder.
bugs.evidence: cycle19/step28-notebooks-person-param.png — all 7 lab-member folders visible, all collapsed, no indication that ?person=vianney-ahn was meaningful. Programmatic: searchValue='', showsVianney=true, showsOtherFolders=true.
bugs.root_cause: init() in notebooks.html only read the 'doc' URL param; 'person' was silently dropped. Logged as a known bug in cycle 18.

bugs.summary: PROTOCOLS-EMPTY-STATE-MINIMAL + NOTEBOOKS-EMPTY-STATE-MINIMAL — two sidebar 0-result states rendered plain-text 'No protocols match' / 'No notebooks match' instead of the styled .empty-state with the search_off icon used on inventory/waste.
bugs.evidence: cycle19/step09-protocols-search-typo.png + cycle19/step15-notebooks-search-nomatch.png
bugs.root_cause: protocols.html line 428 and notebooks.html line 481 used ad-hoc inline markup instead of the shared .empty-state class.

bugs.summary: WIKI-GRAPH-NOT-FILTERED — wiki.html sidebar search (#docSearch) filters the folder tree, but the knowledge graph in the main pane stays at full 300+ nodes regardless of the search. When the sidebar reads 'No documents match', the main view is contradictory.
bugs.evidence: cycle19/step19-wiki-search-typo.png — sidebar shows 'No documents match' tiny text, graph still shows all nodes at full opacity.
bugs.root_cause: docSearch event handler only calls renderSidebar; never communicates to the graph renderer. Graph has no filter/dim path for a sidebar query.

fixes: app/people.html: added body.wizard-open class toggle on openOnboardingWizard/closeWizard + CSS rule 'body.wizard-open #issue-reporter-btn { display: none !important; }'. The issue-reporter FAB is now fully hidden during the wizard overlay at all viewport widths.

app/notebooks.html: init() now reads ?person=<slug> param; when present, pre-fills #nbSearch with the prettified slug (e.g. 'Vianney Ahn' for 'vianney-ahn') and renders the sidebar filtered to only that folder. Onboarding wizard's success checklist link now lands users on their own folder.
app/protocols.html: 0-result sidebar empty state now uses the styled .empty-state div with a 48px search_off icon, matching inventory/waste.
app/notebooks.html: same empty-state consistency treatment for the sidebar 0-result state, with a smaller 40px padding to fit the sidebar column.
observations: Search works correctly on all 5 page-level search inputs (inventory, protocols, notebooks, wiki, waste) — no functional bugs, only styling + carry-over items.
XSS input ('<script>alert(1)</script>') is properly escaped in the search box — no alert fires, no DOM injection (invXssAlert=false).
Emoji input (💎) and em-dash (—) render correctly as Unicode in the input and in the empty state.
Typo queries produce correct 0-result empty states on every page after this cycle's cosmetic unification.
Dashboard has no global/site-wide search — confirmed. Each surface owns its own filter. Potential future R-slot to add cross-surface search.

Notebooks Recent Entries (right pane) is independent of the sidebar search — typing into the sidebar does NOT filter Recent Entries. Logged as an observation rather than a bug; the behavior is arguably correct (Recent Entries is globally recent by design).
Wiki sidebar search leaves the main knowledge graph unfiltered even at 0 sidebar matches — bigger change, logged WIKI-GRAPH-NOT-FILTERED as OPEN for a future cycle.
Cycle-19 test artifacts: none created. Verify kept at tests/qa-cycle-19-verify.mjs as a regression harness for all 3 fixes.
No test files need to be deleted from GitHub.

bugs: INV-TYPE-BADGE-HARDCODED-TEAL: every row in the Inventory table rendered its Type column badge in teal regardless of type, making Chemical/Reagent/Kit/Enzyme/Equipment visually indistinguishable. Fixed by switching app/inventory.html line ~653 from '' + typeLabel + '' to inline style from Lab.types.pillStyle(type, 'badge'). Verified post-fix: Chemical=#6A1B9A purple, Reagent=#009688 teal, Kit=#00838F cyan, Enzyme=#2E7D32 green, Consumable=#1565C0 blue, Equipment=#455A64 slate, Buffer/Solution=#E65100 orange — all seven distinct colors.
fixes: Commit 3f1c5a30: color inventory type badge by type instead of hardcoded teal
observations: The purple chemical pill already rendered correctly in object popups (popup header + of-field + breadcrumb) — the bug was only on the Inventory table. This fits the pattern where the canonical type system in types.js is authoritative and local hardcoded classes drift.

BOTTLE-NOT-ROLLED-UP-AFTER-CREATE: right after addInstanceFromConcept + save, the new bottle briefly appears as its own inventory row instead of rolling up under its concept. Reloading the page fixes it — suggesting renderTable runs before bottlesByConcept is rebuilt when the lab-file-saved event fires. Not fixing this cycle; low severity (transient).

End-to-end workflow solid: Add → pick concept → editor → fill fields → save → read-only popup → concept popup lists new bottle → location popup lists new bottle. Full object-graph round-trip works as designed.

bugs.summary: Add Accession modal rendered two Species inputs (addSpecies + addSpeciesField). Only addSpeciesField was read by confirmAdd, so typing into the visually prominent first Species field silently dropped the value on save.
setSort('priority') defaulted to ascending; clicking the star header put 0-star rows at the top instead of floating 3-star rows up.

At 1024x800 the last visible nav tab was partially clipped (Projects -> Proj) because updateOverflow() hid trailing tabs then showed the "+ More" button, which shrank tabWrap further without a second hide pass.

fixes.what: Removed the dead #addSpecies input from the first form-row, leaving #addSpeciesField as the sole Species input. Reordered rows so Project + Species live on one row and People spans the next row alone.
setSort() now treats priority and mtime like last_updated — numeric/recency columns default to descending on first click so the highest-priority rows float to the top.
Refactored updateOverflow() to run the hide-trailing-tabs loop a second time after toggling the "+ More" button visible, so the shrunken tabWrap no longer leaves a partially-clipped trailing tab.

workflows_tested.observations: Fix 3 is the most subtle — you only see it when the viewport is tight enough that hiding N trailing tabs leaves the container just barely oversized once the "+ More" button claims its ~70px of horizontal space. A second pass of the same loop is sufficient. Verify run confirmed 6 tabs visible cleanly at 1024px with Projects moved into the popover. Also confirmed XSS payload and path traversal in ?doc= are safely handled — no alert, no escape, friendly not-found view.

bugs.summary: wiki.html ?doc= page rendered '<Type pill> <slug>' in the edit-bar breadcrumb instead of the frontmatter title. For objects created via the popup editor (body-empty, frontmatter-only) the slug was the only thing visible next to the type pill — e.g. a new box showed 'Box locations/qa22-libprep-kit-storage-modji38x' instead of 'Box QA22 LibPrep Kit Storage modji38x'. Most action-agent popups resolve by type+title, so this inconsistency stood out.

fixes.what: loadDoc() was already parsing frontmatter but only used `typeBadge + Lab.escHtml(docPath)` for the breadcrumb. Updated to prefer `meta.title` as the primary bold label and demote the slug to smaller secondary grey text (`breadcrumbLabel` ternary). Falls back to raw slug when frontmatter has no title.

observations: Box creation flow is tight end-to-end: shelf popup -> +Add -> openNew pre-seeds parent+type+grid, save regenerates the slug from the final title, returnTo flips back to the parent popup. The user never has to touch parent/type. Good UX — only two fields (Name + Grid) typed by hand for a standard box.

patchObjectIndex() is the critical piece that lets freshly-created objects appear immediately in grids, backlinks panes, and tree counts. Without it (the ghPut-only path) there's a ~40s deploy gap where the UI shows empty state. The UI save path already calls it; automated tests that bypass it must call it explicitly to mirror real-user behavior.

The grid cell label_2 text gets truncated in 10x10 tiny cells — 'P4-A1' becomes 'P4-' because only 2 short lines fit. Acceptable; the full label is visible in the tube popup. Would be nice to have a tooltip on hover showing the full label_2, but not critical.

Accession backlinks correctly classify the new QA22 tubes as instances-in-lab via `of: accessions/pistachio-4` (R5 frontmatter-pointer resolver). Sorted alphabetically; no duplicates. The patchObjectIndex write fully covers this path.
wiki.html doc header, pre-fix, was a single surface that skipped the frontmatter title. editor-modal popups have always shown the title prominently. With the fix the two are now visually consistent.

workflows_tested.observations: Adversarial inputs surfaced 2 new issues plus surfaced an opportunity to sweep an open carry-over. All Lab.escHtml-using surfaces (table cells, peopleCellHtml, statusPillHtml, priorityStarsHtml) handled HTML correctly. The only escape-leakage was in the auto-generated body markdown that confirmAdd writes — a sanitizer there closed it. The table overflow was a long-standing CSS gap (no max-width or word-break on ID td) that no real-life accession had exposed because real accession IDs are short. 0 dialogs fired = no XSS execution at any point.

bugs.summary: WIKI-GRAPH-NOT-FILTERED — wiki.html sidebar search and type-chip filters update the folder tree but the welcome-pane force-directed graph kept showing all 903 nodes at full color regardless of search state.
bugs.evidence: /tmp/qa-screenshots/cycle25/step12-wiki-search-qa25.png shows sidebar filtered to 'QA25' matches but the graph dense cluster unchanged. Carryover from cycle 19.
bugs.root_cause: createForceGraph built nodes from ALL_DOCS once at page load and the docSearch input handler only triggered renderSidebar() — there was no path from filter state to the graph's draw loop.

fixes: Added matchSet support to createForceGraph: an optional Set<slug> dims nodes (and their connecting edges) when set. Exposed setMatchSet() on the graph instance. Added computeWikiMatchSet() / applyWikiGraphMatch() helpers that walk the same TREE filterNode produces. Wired into docSearch input handler, type chip toggle, and clearFilters. (app/wiki.html, commit 426be61e)
observations: of: picker datalist correctly includes 232 accessions/ entries for type=tube (R20 #165 fix is holding). For bottles it's resources/+stocks/ only; for sample/extraction/library/pool it's accessions/ only; for tubes it's the union (accessions+samples+resources+stocks).

End-to-end of:-picker → save → backlinks loop works without deploy: patchLinkIndex inserts the new wikilink targets immediately, so the accession popup CONTENTS pane shows the new tubes as instances-in-lab the moment the save finishes.

openNew with parent + position pre-fills the Parent display text (full breadcrumb 'Robbins Hall 170 > -80C Freezer A > Shelf 1 > Pistachio DNA Box') and the Position field (B5) — the user only needs to fill Name and Of.

After R22 the slug regenerates from the final title at save time — so the file lands at qa25-pistachio-aliquot-1-RUN.md instead of new-tube-RUN.md. Verification scripts that look for new-tube-* by name will miss the actual file; cleanup should match by RUN tag inside file body or by the resolved title.
5-level breadcrumb chain (Room/Freezer/Shelf/Box/Tube) renders cleanly in the tube popup with type-colored pills.
Of pill upgrade (cycle-4 fix b0da836) still holds: instance popups show the concept's resolved title + correct type-color rather than the raw slug.

Cosmetic carryover: cells in a 10×10 grid only fit ~4 chars of label, so 3 distinct tubes show identical 'QA25' label fragments. Position is the disambiguator. Acceptable; full title visible on click.

workflows_tested.task: Aliquot day with impatient race-condition stress. Grad student aliquots accession C127_1 (cassava) into 4 tubes at A1/A2/B1/B2 of the existing DNA Extracts Box (8x12 in fridge-4c-main). Each tube created via openNew() with double-click on Save to test for duplicate-file creation. Then a 5th tube was started and the page was refreshed mid-edit to test impatient interruption. Verified: no duplicates from double-click (4 expected files = 4 actual), no orphan from interrupted edit, lab map shows complete hierarchy with position pills, accession popup correctly lists 4 instances in lab.

bugs.description: Accession popup Priority field shows raw "2" instead of ★★ stars. Mismatched the Accessions tracker table cell which already renders stars.
Accession popup People field shows "Grey Monroe / Kehan Zhao" as plain text instead of individual pills (popup lagged the cycle 26 table-cell fix).

In renderBacklinksPane, instance subtitles displayed parent slug-derived text ("box dna extracts") instead of the parent title ("DNA Extracts Box"). Source: editor-modal.js renderBacklinksPane line 1966 used `b.parent.split("/").pop().replace(/-/g, " ")` without an index lookup.

Notebooks landing first recent entry "Vianney Ahn / Fiberseq" shows "MN" as subtitle instead of date YYYY-MM-DD. Other rows show dates correctly. Likely the entry has a non-date metadata value being read for the date column.
New Tube edit form, Of (concept slug) field placeholder reads "e.g. accessions/pistachio-4 or re..." — truncated, should be "or resources/...".
Accession popup Detail Sheet Link field shows truncated URL as plain text ("https://docs.google.com/doc..."), not rendered as a clickable hyperlink.

fixes.message: qa-cycle-27: popup field rendering — priority stars, people pills, instance parent title

observations: Impatient double-click on Save did NOT create duplicate files — single click protection holds (likely due to async save being idempotent within the same currentState).
Refreshing the page mid-edit does NOT create an orphan file. openNew() only writes to GitHub on explicit Save click. This confirms the createOverlay/state-only contract.
Existing collision-detection at grid cell A1 displays a "2" badge when two tubes share the same position — useful UX.
Lab Map filter by tag walks the full hierarchy (room → freezer → shelf → box → tube) and renders position pills cleanly.
Verified the popup fix on the deployed live site (post-deploy 230d4fde) before cleanup.

workflows_tested.scenario: Equipment move day. Postdoc Jordan in rushed mode created 3 new bottles of [[resources/ethanol-absolute]] (chemical concept, purple pill) on the Flammable Cabinet, then moved all 3 to the Chemical Cabinet via the editor's locations picker. Verified the source-popup loses children, the destination-popup gains them, and the lab-map tree reflects the move. Also exercised: the [+ Add item] button on the cabinet popup col 3, the parent picker tree (collapsed-by-default with chevron expand), wikilink concept->bottle backlinks. Cycle re-verified that bottles are stored under docs/stocks/ (not docs/resources/) — earlier path lookup miss caused the move-step to be re-run via /tmp/qa-cycle-28-continue.mjs.

screenshots.evaluation: Repro confirms POPUP-BREADCRUMB-RACE-AFTER-SAVE fix works. Without the fix, parentChain would return [] for the unknown slug and crumbHTML stays empty. With the fix, when chain.length<2 AND meta.parent is present, patchObjectIndex is called to inject the just-loaded meta into the cache; hierarchy.invalidate fires inside patchObjectIndex, and the next parentChain call resolves the chain. ✓

bugs.summary: Breadcrumb missing on popup of a freshly-saved/created object until a hard reload (because cached object-index + hierarchy graph predate the new entry).

fixes.summary: In openPopup breadcrumb branch: if parentChain returns chain.length<2 but the just-loaded meta has a `parent:` field, patchObjectIndex(filePath, parsed.meta) to inject the meta into the in-memory cache and invalidate hierarchy, then retry parentChain once. Renders breadcrumb on first popup view rather than waiting for a reload.

observations: INSTANCE_TYPES classification (editor-modal.js:1997) correctly counts both `of:`-driven and body-wikilink-driven instances. R19 tubes that reference an accession only via body [[wikilinks]] (no `of:` field) still get classified as 'instances in lab' rather than 'references'.
Chemical (#6a1b9a fa-atom) vs Reagent (#009688 fa-flask) pill colors render distinctly on Inventory rows AND inside the popup field-pane Of-slug pill — the recolor survives the modal context.

Cross-linker walk path verified: inventory list -> chemical popup -> 1-instance-bottle-row click -> bottle popup with parent pill + 3-pill breadcrumb -> can pivot to accession side -> tube popup with 4-pill breadcrumb (room/freezer/shelf/box) -> body wikilinks render as clickable pills with correct icon+color per object type. No broken chain link encountered at 1440 viewport.

POPUP-BREADCRUMB-RACE-AFTER-SAVE root cause: editor-modal.js:971 awaits hierarchy.parentChain before the new file appears in the cached object-index. Hierarchy.build() short-circuits to cached _graph until invalidate() fires. patchObjectIndex (which is called inside the editor-save flow) does call invalidate, but external creation paths (direct GitHub API, qa-tests, race conditions where user opens popup before save commit returns) bypass it. The fix lazily patches in the just-loaded meta only when needed, so legitimate root objects (chain.length===1 with no parent) still skip the breadcrumb without retry.

screenshots.what_it_shows: Same screen as 09 but priority is now ★★☆ and Updated is 2026-04-25 — my td:nth(0).click() landed on the priority cell (which has its own click handler with stopPropagation), so the click cycled priority instead of opening the popup.
screenshots.evaluation: Working as designed: clicking on the star cell cycles priority and intentionally does NOT open the popup (event.stopPropagation in the cell's onclick). The row's open-popup handler is intentionally suppressed for priority and other inline-editable cells. The click should have targeted td:nth(1) (the ID cell) to open the popup.
issues_found: UX: A user scanning a row visually may aim for the first column without realizing it's a star toggle. Consider adding a subtle 'click stars to set priority' tooltip on hover, or shifting the priority column to the right of the ID column. Not a regression — but the affordance is implicit. Logged as cosmetic UX nit, not fixed this cycle.

issues_found: MINOR: at the bottom-right of the viewport the Connections panel, the lightbulb FAB (issue reporter), and the hamburger button are stacked close together — the lightbulb partially overlaps the Pistachio DNA Box node in the small graph. Cosmetic, not blocking.

observations: Wiki deep-link error handling is rock solid: fake doc, path-traversal, weird chars, and deleted doc all show the same graceful 'Document not found' UI — no JS exceptions, no infinite spinners, no filesystem leaks.
Empty ?doc= param correctly falls through to the welcome graph (loadDoc skipped, showWelcome called).

wiki.html `loadDoc` cache-fallback (60s lab_file_cache) correctly masks GitHub's ~30s eventual-consistency for fresh creates AND can be busted by clearing localStorage so that deletes propagate immediately.

Connections panel currently undercounts: it does not include `of:` frontmatter pointers from instance objects, only body wikilinks. After the GH Actions index rebuild (~1 min) the panel does populate from body wikilinks; the screenshot 03 'No wikilinks yet' was strictly index-lag.

Accessions table row-click → openAccession() is wired correctly, BUT the priority cell stops propagation (by design) and cycles priority on click. A user (or test) clicking the leftmost cell will cycle priority instead of opening the popup. Not a regression — but the affordance is implicit. Considered fixing by moving the click-to-edit affordance to a dedicated star icon, but the current model (whole-cell click) is intentional and matches the inline-edit UX.

Lab map tree starts collapsed at the room level — Pistachio DNA Box is several layers deep. The '10 items' badge on a parent row is helpful but a deep-search auto-expand would help find specific containers without manual drilling. Future enhancement, not filed as bug.

Test side-effect: my Playwright `td:nth(0).click()` landed on the priority star and cycled A1-T1-E priority twice (1→2→3 stars) and bumped last_updated to 2026-04-25. Restored via direct GitHub API write at end of run. Did not leave permanent state changes in the docs.

workflows_tested.observations: Per-page search surfaces are independent (no global cross-page search). Inventory/accessions/protocols/notebooks all auto-expand or surface hits inline; wiki was the lone outlier where filtered categories rendered collapsed and required an extra click per category. Comment in wiki renderNode claimed "Auto-expand top level (depth 0) when there is an active filter" but the code never honored it — fixed. Pill colors correct end-to-end across pages: chemical purple, reagent teal, buffer/solution orange, kit teal, equipment green, protocol purple, accession purple, person grey-blue. Stat cards on inventory/accessions stay populated under active filter (intended). Notebook search appears title-only (does not match body content like NanoDrop in qa34 mobile entry) — consistent with protocols search behavior, not flagged as bug.

workflows_tested.task: Reorder day scenario. Undergrad in a hurry checks inventory on the live site, picks the first 3 in_stock items (1,8-Naphthalic anhydride, 1-HYDROXY-3-(3-TRIFLUOROMETHYLPHENYL)UREA, 1-Octanol — all `chemical` purple-pill items in chemical/flammable cabinets), rapid-fires status changes via the Lab.setStatus() inline select on each row (no UI delay between toggles, just enough wait for each GitHub commit), reloads to verify in-memory stat counts vs reload counts, hops over to the dashboard to confirm Inventory Status needs_more count goes from 2 → 5, then reverts ONE item (1,8-Naphthalic) back to in_stock and verifies count decrements to 4 on both inventory page and dashboard. Cleaned up the remaining 2 toggled items at end of run. Caught one visible bug on the dashboard alert list (long-name pill wrap), fixed and verified.

observations: Reorder day workflow round-trips correctly: in-memory stat counts updated optimistically by setStatus(), reload counts match, dashboard inventoryStatus reflects the new counts on next page load.

setStatus() commits each change to GitHub individually — rapid impatient clicks burn one Actions minute per click. Per #124, the inline select is intentional (vs the old click-to-cycle that fired multiple commits per intended state).

OBSERVATION-36-A: locationCell falls back to badge-grey for free-text locations not present in locationsBySlug (e.g. 'Bench' for Microcentrifuge). Visually distinct from the typed location pill (icon + title) used for chemical-cabinet rows. Intentional per code comment but worth noting for design consistency.
Toast queue handles multiple back-to-back success toasts cleanly.
Cleanup script reverted the 2 still-toggled items to in_stock at end of run; final stats (218/2/1) match pre-cycle baseline. No data drift.

workflows_tested.scenario: Star priority sort + accession lifecycle on mobile
workflows_tested.task: Postdoc Jordan working from a 375x812 phone viewport opens accessions.html, scans the orientation banner, the 7-tile stats grid, and the mobile card list. Programmatically sorts by priority, taps the priority stars on the top card (J4_T1_5 Kerman ★★★), expects the priority to cycle 3→0 — instead the editor modal opens. Identifies and fixes the root cause: the .pri-stars click handler was attached only to #tableBody children, leaving the .acc-cards (mobile) stars without event.stopPropagation, so tap propagated to the card wrapper's onclick=openAccession(). Also notices the stats grid orphans 'Archived' alone in the last row when the Add Accession tile is visible (7 cards in a 2-col grid). Fixes both in app/accessions.html (commit 52dc8654). Re-runs verify post-deploy: priority cycles 3→0 cleanly with no modal, Archived spans both cols (343px width).

observations: Mobile card view on accessions.html (≤768px) hides the table and renders .acc-card tiles instead. The card layout has stars + name + status pill on top, people pills + project + species on bottom.

OBSERVATION-37-A (open UX gap): mobile card view has no sort dropdown / button — desktop table headers are the only sort affordance and they are hidden on mobile. Users on a phone cannot trigger sort by priority / last_updated / etc.

OBSERVATION-37-B (re-confirmed from cycle 4, open): the blue lightbulb issue-reporter FAB at bottom:80px right:18px overlaps the right edge of mobile cards (and the right side of the body in the single-column accession popup). Affects every page with a list on mobile. Out of scope this cycle.
Bottom tab bar (Wiki / Notebooks / Accessions / Inventory / More) is present on mobile and the Accessions tab is correctly highlighted (teal).
Orientation banner renders with a thick purple left border on mobile as expected per the run_focus.
Accession popup on mobile collapses to a single column cleanly — fields on top, body below, Close/Delete/Edit footer reachable. Type pill colors stay correct (purple for accession concept).

Priority cycle path: tap stars → cyclePriority(slug) → fetchFile + saveFile + patchObjectIndex → renderTable. Commit lag is ~2-5s under network conditions; the test polled data-pri to detect the change.
Star tap fix wires the listener to BOTH `#tableBody .pri-stars` and `#cardView .pri-stars` via document.querySelectorAll, so the same stopPropagation behavior applies on both views.

Stats grid orphan fix: tag the .acc-stats container with `has-add-tile` only when the Add tile is shown (logged-in path), and span the last child across both cols in the mobile media query. Without auth (Add tile hidden) the grid has 6 cards in 2 cols = 3 even rows, no orphan, and the rule is not applied (class missing).

workflows_tested.scenario: Accession status overhaul on mobile + editor modal mobile body editor
workflows_tested.task: PI Dr. Monroe on a 375x812 phone opens accessions.html, filters status=storage (baseline 0), searches A3-T2-W, taps the card to open the editor modal, switches to edit mode, changes status active->storage, raises priority 1->3, appends [[people/vianney-ahn]] to people, sets status_note='Stored as reference DNA pool — 2026-04-25 (cycle38 QA)', saves. Reloads, filters storage, verifies the row is there with the new fields. Caught two real bugs in the process: (1) startEditing() in editor-modal.js called setupMobileToolbarToggle(editorEl, editor) with a bare 'editor' identifier that is not declared in that scope — ReferenceError fired immediately on Edit click, and the mobile FAB bar (X / Save / Aa / +) never mounted, leaving the body editor blank below the fields panel. (2) renderFields() builds the status pill in view mode from a hardcoded statusColors map that only knew the inventory/waste statuses (in_stock/needs_more/out_of_stock/external/in_accumulation/ready_for_pickup/picked_up); accession statuses (active/waiting/storage/completed/archived) fell through to default grey #6b7280, so the popup pill rendered grey while the same row in the tracker table rendered amber/blue/green/etc. Fixed both in app/js/editor-modal.js (commit d1fb7dbf): editor->currentEditor on line 2676, and extended statusColors+statusLabels with the 5 accession statuses mapping to the same colors as accessions.html STATUS_COLORS. Verify pass post-deploy: the modal pill now renders rgb(59,130,246) for storage and rgb(245,158,11) for active; toast UI mounts (toastui-editor-defaultUI present), mobile FAB bar mounts (mobile-fab-bar present), 0 page errors. Reverted A3-T2-W back to its original active / 1 / Zi Ye-Matt Davis / 'DNA extracted — Needs Clean-up' state.
