bugs: TUBE-AS-CONTAINER (visual, fixed in cycle 41 — commit ce5dcb09): in the editor-modal backlinks pane, freshly-created tubes/bottles/etc. that weren't yet in object-index.json fell through to type='container' fallback, showing the box-cube icon and 'Container' subtitle. Fixed by inferring type from slug prefix (tube-, bottle-, sample-, extraction-, library-, pool-) and humanizing the slug title.

LABEL-MISMATCH (visual, fixed in cycle 41 — commit ce5dcb09): right column heading was hardcoded as 'Contents' for every popup, including concept popups (accessions, chemicals) where the pane actually shows backlinks. Now switches to 'References' with a link icon when the backlinks branch renders, resets to 'Contents' on every popup open.
fixes: ce5dcb09 — app/js/editor-modal.js: fetchBacklinksFor infers tube/bottle/sample/extraction/library/pool from the leaf prefix and humanizes the slug for the title; renderContents now sets the column heading to References+link icon when rendering the backlinks pane and resets to Contents+inventory_2 on each openPopup.
observations: Lab-map filtered tree (screenshot 12) is the strongest UI in this flow — the same 6 tubes that misrender in the popup show with proper icons and position pills here. The data is consistent; only the popup rendering was the problem.

Accession popup correctly classified all 6 tubes as concept-instances (Add Instance button visible) but rendered them in the wrong group ('References') and with wrong subtitles. Suggests the Add-Instance gate works off of the parent type (currentState.meta.type isConceptType), independent from the per-row classification — the two should agree.

URL deep-linking via ?slug=accessions/<slug> on accessions.html and lab-map.html does NOT auto-open a popup. Worth tracking as a potential UX feature: cross-page links could deep-link if the receiving page parsed ?slug= and opened the popup, like notebooks already does for ?doc=. Not blocking.

GitHub Pages deploy queues commits with cancel-in-progress=false, so 7 back-to-back commits (1 box + 6 tubes) take ~7 deploys to fully drain. The 60s wait in the cycle script wasn't enough to fully refresh object-index — which is exactly the window where the new fix matters most.

bugs_found: BACKLINKS-LAG (functional, fixed in cycle 1): newly-created or freshly-edited files were missing from the backlinks panel of every wikilink target until the next deploy

HIERARCHY-CACHE-STALE (functional, fixed in cycle 2): after a save the Lab.hierarchy cache retained its pre-save snapshot, so parent CONTENTS + child breadcrumbs were missing for new objects until reload

STALE-DASHBOARD-INDEX (functional, fixed in cycle 3): dashboard Inventory Status card did raw fetch of object-index.json, bypassing the localStorage patch overlay — counts + alerts lagged until site redeploy

OF-FIELD-NOT-CLICKABLE (functional, fixed in cycle 4): the `of` field on bottle/tube popups rendered as plain slug text while the `parent` field was a clickable object pill — cross_linker could not navigate instance → concept from the fields column

WASTE-FIELDS-NOT-IN-INDEX-KEYS (functional, fixed in cycle 5): client INDEX_KEYS did not include the waste-container fields (contents, physical_state, container, hazard_class, started, waste_tag) or sample-tracker fields (sample_id, species, lead, sequencing_type) that the server-side EXTRACT_KEYS has, so patchObjectIndex dropped them — new waste containers showed blank Contents/State/Days Held until the next deploy and filters excluded them

FETCHTREE-NO-OVERLAY (functional, fixed in cycle 6): fetchTree returned GitHub's git/trees view directly with no localStorage overlay, so the notebooks/wiki/protocols sidebars showed pre-save folder counts after a reload until the API caught up (~30s)

NOTEBOOKS-SAVE-NO-PATCH (functional, fixed in cycle 6): notebooks.html saveDoc/createDoc/deleteDoc never called patchObjectIndex / patchLinkIndex / removeFrom*, so notebook saves bypassed the overlays — backlinks on wikilink targets did not include the new entry until the next deploy

TABLE-HEADER-HOVER-COLOR (cosmetic, closed): Actions column in inventory.html picked up the teal :hover color that only makes sense on sortable columns; scoped the teal hover to th[onclick] and set cursor:default on the Actions header so the affordance matches behavior (CLOSED in a5eca40)
TOAST-LINGERS (cosmetic, fixed in cycle 8, commit be436a0): success toasts faded after 3s, errors after 6s, all toasts click-to-dismiss — Lab.showToast now picks duration by type

TOPNAV-LAST-ITEM-CLIPPED (cosmetic, fixed in cycle 8, commit ed22c7c): top-nav tab padding tightened from 12px 14px to 12px 10px (and 12px 7px below 1280px) so all 12 tabs fit at 1440-wide without clipping — nav.js
SEARCH-PLACEHOLDER-CLIPPED (cosmetic, fixed in cycle 8, commit dc5bf39): notebook sidebar search placeholder shortened from 'Search notebooks...' to 'Search...' — notebooks.html

STATUS-RAW-SLUG-IN-POPUP (cosmetic, closed): editor popup for a waste container showed Status='in_accumulation' as raw slug text; the editor-modal view-mode renderer now title-cases unknown status slugs and has explicit entries for waste statuses (CLOSED in 28aa44b)

WIKI-404-FOR-UNSAVED-DEPLOY (ux, closed): freshly-created wiki docs 404'd on GitHub's contents API for up to ~30s after save; loadDoc in wiki.html now falls back to the lab_file_cache localStorage overlay (same pattern editor-modal uses) and prefers cached content when it's <60s old (CLOSED in 94f1098)
NAV-TRUNCATION (cosmetic, fixed in cycle 8, commit ed22c7c): same fix as TOPNAV-LAST-ITEM-CLIPPED — tab padding tightened in nav.js so 'Lab Map' fits fully

MAP-PLACEHOLDER-OVERLAP (cosmetic, closed): 'Map design in progress…' label sat at y=95/108 inside the bench rows; moved it into the empty band between Bench 3 and Door (y=158) as a single line over a white rect so it no longer overlaps the bench text (CLOSED in ef3bdf8)

BREADCRUMB-DANGLING-SLASH (cosmetic, fixed in cycle 7): popup breadcrumb wrapped to two lines and left a standalone '/' at line-end because each separator was an independent flex child — fix groups separator+pill inside inline-flex so the pair wraps together

DNA-QUANTIFICATION-PILL-UNTYPED (minor, closed as not-a-bug): investigated in cycle 9 — the slug 'dna-quantification-qubit' doesn't exist in docs/ (the real protocol is 'quantifying-dna-qubit' under wet-lab/). The plain-teal-pill fallback rendering for an unresolved slug is the CORRECT behavior; there's no type in the index so no type pill can be inferred. The underlying problem was a typo in a cycle-7 test box body, not an app bug.

DASHBOARD-TOTAL-VS-STATUS-MISMATCH (cosmetic/ux, closed): loadStats folds untyped objects into 'resource' for the top card while loadInventoryStatus skips them, leaving ~253 items unaccounted. Added an 'Untracked' pill to the status summary counting objects that pass the top card's rule but fail isInv — the three pills now sum to the top-card total (CLOSED in ab3e9c0)

DUPLICATE-TITLE-IN-CONTENTS (cosmetic, closed): contents column of a location popup listed children with identical titles identically; renderChildrenListPane now detects sibling title collisions and appends a lightweight subtitle (lot+expiration, or slug tail) only on colliding rows (CLOSED in 9f159da)

FAB-OVERLAPS-TABLE (visual, fixed in cycle 8, commit 5f2e1ff): moved issue-reporter FAB from bottom-left to bottom-right at 80px — clears the leftmost inventory/waste column, sits above the toast container so neither covers the other

ROOM-BREADCRUMB-REDUNDANT (cosmetic, closed): for root locations the breadcrumb was a single self-pill that just echoed the H1 title below it; editor-modal now checks parentChain length and skips the breadcrumb when length === 1, in both the initial openPopup render and the post-save re-render (CLOSED in dbd4df7)

FILTER-SUBSTRING-ONLY (minor, closed): lab-map applyFilter in location-tree.js now prefers word-boundary matches (regex checks for start/space/hyphen/underscore/slash before the query). Falls back to substring matches when no word-boundary hit exists so partial prefixes still return results (CLOSED in ef3bdf8)

OF-PILL-TRUNCATED-NO-ELLIPSIS (visual, fixed in cycle 8, commit 0088518): Of/Parent pills in bottle/tube popup fields column clipped long object titles mid-word because the pill anchor was inline-flex with white-space:nowrap and no max-width; wrapping span lacked min-width:0 + flex:1. Fix adds min-width:0/flex:1 on the placeholder, max-width:100%/min-width:0 on the upgraded anchor, and ellipsis on a child span that holds the title; icon stays visible. title="" attribute added for full-title hover tooltip

CONCEPT-HAS-PARENT-BREADCRUMB (data-quality, closed in cycle 9, commits f7cfe1f + a92e314): (a) deleted the two remaining stale LabBot concept fixtures that carried a legacy parent:/position: frontmatter (labbot-freezer-item-mnthp11v, tube-item) and (b) added a CONCEPT_TYPES guard in app/js/hierarchy.js build() — concept-type objects (reagent/chemical/buffer/solution/consumable/kit/enzyme/primer) now ignore their parent: field, so they don't pollute any container's Contents list and don't render a physical breadcrumb even if legacy frontmatter still has parent: set.

CALENDAR-NO-INDEX-PATCH (functional, fixed in cycle 9, commit 8a679ba): newly-created calendar events disappeared from the calendar grid after page reload until the static object-index.json rebuilt on deploy. openAddEvent called gh.saveFile but never called patchObjectIndex or patchLinkIndex, so the localStorage overlays had nothing to merge on reload. Fix calls both overlays after saveFile (type=event + date/time/member/recurrence_id/created_by for index, member+notes wikilinks for link-index) and adds recurrence_id to client INDEX_KEYS to mirror the python EXTRACT_KEYS (cycle-5 pattern).

TOPNAV-WIKI-CLIPPED (cosmetic, closed in cycle 10, commit d8c96cc): at 1440px the 'Wiki' tab label clipped to 'Wik' after the user avatar+signout fit on the right. Tightened desktop tab padding from 12px 10px to 12px 8px with gap 4px, and narrow-desktop (769-1280px) rule from 12px 7px to 12px 6px with gap 3px, so all 12 tabs fit at 1440 including the full 'Wiki' label. Verified at 1440 width — full label visible with no overflow scrollbar (app/js/nav.js).

MEMBER-FIELD-RAW-WIKILINK (ux, closed in cycle 10, commit d8c96cc): Member(s) field on an event popup rendered `[[Dr. Monroe]]` as raw wikilink text, not as a clickable Person pill. Added a member-field branch to renderFields in editor-modal.js that emits data-member-pill placeholders for each [[wikilink]] in the value, and upgradeParentField now also handles the member-pill placeholders. Falls back to a title-match lookup against the cached object index so display-name wikilinks like [[Dr. Monroe]] resolve to the Grey Monroe person card even though the slug differs (app/js/editor-modal.js).

MEMBER-COLOR-SHUFFLE (ux, minor, closed in cycle 10, commit d8c96cc): Member color assignment was alphabetical by index, so adding a new member rotated existing members' colors. Replaced the index-lookup with a djb2 hash of the member name modulo palette size so each member maps to the same color regardless of who else is in the schedule. New members no longer reshuffle existing event colors (app/calendar.html).

DOUBLE-FRONTMATTER-BAR (cosmetic, fixed in cycle 11): print-preview overlay showed two 'Protocol' pills because togglePrintPreview hid #renderedDoc but left .proto-content > .frontmatter-bar visible above the iframe, while showPrintPreview deliberately copies the parent's frontmatter bar HTML into the iframe so it gets paginated. Added body.print-preview-active rule + extended @media print rule in app/protocols.html to hide .proto-content > .frontmatter-bar so only the iframe's copy remains.

FAB-NOT-HIDDEN-IN-PRINT (visual, fixed in cycle 11): #print-preview-fab and #issue-reporter-btn were not in the parent's @media print { display:none } list, so a Cmd+P from the parent would print both FABs on every page. Added both ids to the existing @media print rule in app/protocols.html.

PROTOCOL-IMAGES-BROKEN (cosmetic, PARTIALLY FIXED in cycle 12, commit 908cf5d — 1 of 5 swapped): pipette-tips Wikimedia hotlink replaced with real local 170KB JPEG in docs/images/pipette-tips-in-boxes.jpg. Remaining 4 Wikimedia thumbnails (gel-electrophoresis annotated, gel UV transilluminator, micropipettes on stand, forward pipetting instruction) still hotlink to upload.wikimedia.org and will 429 intermittently. Local stubs in docs/images/ are 1.9KB HTML error pages — untracked, don't ship. Needs a lab member to drop real photos for the remaining 4.

HEADING-EATS-IMAGE (visual+functional, fixed in cycle 12, commit e7d8ea5): resized images inserted after an H2 heading got fused into the heading in saved markdown. applyImageSizes now wraps the replacement with blank lines and collapses excess newlines.

INVENTORY-LOCATION-RAW-SLUG (visual, fixed in cycle 13): Inventory table LOCATION column rendered raw slugs like 'locations/cabinet-flammable' inside plain grey badges instead of typed pills with resolved title+icon. Fix: app/inventory.html now builds a locationsBySlug lookup at load time and renders each cell via a new locationCell(loc) helper that uses Lab.types.pillStyle + pillContent when the slug resolves, falling back to a plain grey badge for free-text, multi-location aggregates, or unresolved slugs. Applied to both the desktop table and mobile card view.

INSTANCE-TITLES-AMBIGUOUS (minor, logged in cycle 13, NOT fixed): on a concept popup the CONTENTS -> instances list shows each bottle as title+subtitle(parent). When multiple bottles share a concept AND the same parent (e.g. two Ethanol 70% bottles both on 'bench'), rows look identical. Lot number or slug tail in subtitle would help; low priority.

NAV-NOT-HIDDEN-IN-MOBILE-EDIT (ux/visual, fixed in cycle 14, commit 932aa37): at mobile viewport (≤768px) the notebook editing-mode CSS rules for #lab-nav and #lab-bottom-nav lacked !important, so inline display:flex from nav.js (and the @media display:flex!important on #lab-bottom-nav) won. Edit mode left both nav bars visible, shrinking the editor to roughly 70% of the screen. Fix adds !important to both display:none rules in notebooks.html and also zeros the body padding-bottom that nav.js injects on mobile so the editor reclaims the full viewport.

FAB-COVERS-MOBILE-SHEET (visual, fixed in cycle 14, commit 932aa37): the mobile Insert bottom sheet used z-index:9998 with a 9997 backdrop, but the issue-reporter lightbulb FAB sits at 10001 (raised above editor-modal per R7 #33). At 375px the FAB punched through the sheet and covered the Calendar action row. Fix bumps sheet to 10050 and backdrop to 10049 in editor-modal.js so the sheet paints above the FAB and the backdrop dims it while open.

RENAME-DOUBLE-CLICK-FAILURE-TOAST (functional/ux, fixed in cycle 15, commit c3fb6b6): double-clicking the Rename button on a protocol fires a spurious 'Rename failed: Not Found' red toast even though the first click succeeded (new title + new URL both applied). Cause: renameDoc had no re-entry guard; both synchronous clicks entered the function before the first await, and the second call's fetchFile 404s because the first click already deleted the old file. Fix: renameInFlight flag set before the first await and cleared in a finally block (app/protocols.html).

DOUBLE-LOAD-ERROR-PREFIX (cosmetic/ux, fixed in cycle 15, commit c3fb6b6): loadDoc error UI in protocols/wiki/projects/notebooks rendered 'Failed to load: Failed to load docs/<path>.md (HTTP 404)' — duplicated 'Failed to load' prefix (UI wrapper + thrown error message), raw file path leak, and exposed HTTP status. Fix: new Lab.formatLoadError(err) helper in app/js/shared.js strips the prefix and maps 404s to 'Document not found. It may have been renamed or deleted.'; applied across all four pages.

SEARCH-PROTOCOL-PLACEHOLDER-CLIPPED (cosmetic, fixed in cycle 15, commit c3fb6b6): protocols sidebar search placeholder 'Search protocols...' was clipped to 'Search protocol' because the input competes with two adjacent icon buttons for horizontal space. Shortened to 'Search...' (mirrors cycle-8 notebooks fix).

BULK-BAR-STALE-AFTER-EMPTY-RENDER (visual/ux, fixed in cycle 17): after bulk-delete of all filtered rows on /sample-tracker/, the bulk action bar remained visible with stale '10 selected' text. Root cause: renderTable's early return for empty items skipped updateBulkBar(). Fix: call updateBulkBar() in both branches.

STATUS-SELECT-CLIPS-LONG-LABEL (visual, fixed in cycle 17): 'Sequencing in progress' status pill clipped to 'Sequencing in pr' inside the inline <select> on /sample-tracker/. Fix: min-width: 168px on .inline-select.status-select so the longest status fits.

PEOPLE-CARD-PILLS-BARE-SLUG (functional, fixed in cycle 18, commit 52fcfd3): every connected pill on every person card used ?doc=<bare-slug>, so any protocol or project living in a subfolder (wet-lab/autoclave, library-prep/*, epigenomics/*, etc.) 404'd with 'Document not found' on click. Fix resolves wl.slug against the cached object-index and uses obj.path (minus .md, minus /index) for the href. Verified at cycle18-verify/02 — clicking the autoclave pill on a pre-existing person card now loads the full autoclave protocol.

PERSON-DASHBOARD-IGNORES-FORWARD-LINKS (functional/ux, fixed in cycle 18, commit 52fcfd3): the person dashboard aggregated INBOUND backlinks only (items wikilinking TO people/<slug>). The onboarding wizard writes OUTBOUND wikilinks (person body -> protocols/projects), so newly-onboarded users landed on a 0-across-the-board dashboard. Fix merges outbound edges from the link-index plus a fallback body-parse into backlinkedItems before categorizing into sections. Verified: fresh-user dashboard now shows 1 Notebook / 2 Protocols / 1 Project with expandable section rows.

NOTEBOOK-ENTRY-NOT-PATCHED (functional, fixed in cycle 18, commit 52fcfd3): wizard's createPersonFiles patchObjectIndex'd only the person card, not the first notebook entry, so the dashboard's Lab Notebooks filter (path startsWith notebooks/<slug>/) read 0 until the next site deploy. Fix also patches the entry path with {type:'notebook', title:'<date> — <name>'}. Verified with notebookCount=1 immediately after onboarding.

NOTEBOOKS-PERSON-PARAM-IGNORED (ux, closed in cycle 19, commit 1e90ba8): notebooks.html init() now reads ?person=<slug> and pre-fills #nbSearch with the prettified slug so the sidebar renders only that person's folder. Onboarding wizard's 'Write your first lab notebook entry' checklist link now lands users on their own folder. Verified via tests/qa-cycle-19-verify.mjs — showsVianney=true, showsOtherFolders=false.

FAB-NEAR-WIZARD-NEXT-MOBILE / FAB-OVERLAPS-WIZARD-NEXT-MOBILE (visual, closed in cycle 19, commit 1e90ba8): cycle 19 measurement confirmed actual overlap (not just proximity) — FAB {right:357} over Next {right:347} at 375px. Fix: people.html toggles body.wizard-open on openOnboardingWizard/closeWizard, and a new 'body.wizard-open #issue-reporter-btn { display: none !important; }' CSS rule hides the FAB while the overlay is open. Verified: FAB display=none, body has wizard-open class.

PROTOCOLS-EMPTY-STATE-MINIMAL (cosmetic, closed in cycle 19, commit 1e90ba8): protocols sidebar 0-result state was plain '

No protocols match

'; swapped to '.empty-state' div with the search_off icon, matching inventory/waste.

NOTEBOOKS-EMPTY-STATE-MINIMAL (cosmetic, closed in cycle 19, commit 1e90ba8): notebooks sidebar 0-result state used inline ad-hoc styling; swapped to the standard .empty-state block with the search_off icon plus a '+' hint to create one.

WIKI-GRAPH-NOT-FILTERED (ux, OPEN from cycle 19): wiki.html sidebar search (#docSearch) filters the folder tree but the knowledge graph in the main pane stays unchanged even when the sidebar reads 'No documents match'. Larger change — logged for a future cycle. Options: filter graph nodes to match the sidebar, dim non-matching nodes, or hide the graph placeholder message when no results.

NOTEBOOKS-RECENT-ENTRIES-NOT-FILTERED (ux, OPEN from cycle 19, debatable): sidebar search does not filter the right-pane Recent Entries list. Behavior may be intentional — Recent Entries is globally recent by design — but a confused user might expect filtering. Logged as an observation rather than a bug.

INV-TYPE-BADGE-HARDCODED-TEAL (visual, closed in cycle 20, commit 3f1c5a30): every row in the Inventory table rendered its Type column badge in teal regardless of type, so Chemical / Reagent / Kit / Enzyme / Equipment / Consumable / Buffer-Solution were visually indistinguishable. Fix: replaced hardcoded 'badge badge-teal' class with inline Lab.types.pillStyle(type, 'badge') in app/inventory.html renderTable. Post-fix: 7 types render 7 distinct colors.

BOTTLE-NOT-ROLLED-UP-AFTER-CREATE (ux, OPEN from cycle 20, low severity): right after addInstanceFromConcept saves a new bottle, it appears as its own Inventory row instead of rolling up under the concept's bottle count. Reloading the page fixes it — suggests the 'lab-file-saved' listener updates the individual row instead of rebuilding bottlesByConcept. Not fixed this cycle; transient.

bugs_found.summary: Add Accession modal rendered two Species inputs (addSpecies + addSpeciesField). Only addSpeciesField was read by confirmAdd, so typing into the visually prominent first Species field silently dropped the value on save.
setSort('priority') defaulted to ascending; clicking the star header put 0-star rows at the top instead of floating 3-star rows up.

At 1024x800 the last visible nav tab was partially clipped (Projects -> Proj) because updateOverflow() hid trailing tabs then showed the "+ More" button, which shrank tabWrap further without a second hide pass.

wiki.html ?doc= page rendered '<Type pill> <slug>' in the edit-bar breadcrumb instead of the frontmatter title. For objects created via the popup editor (body-empty, frontmatter-only) the slug was the only thing visible next to the type pill — e.g. a new box showed 'Box locations/qa22-libprep-kit-storage-modji38x' instead of 'Box QA22 LibPrep Kit Storage modji38x'. Most action-agent popups resolve by type+title, so this inconsistency stood out.

On a fresh browser (no cached fonts), nav.js ran updateOverflow via setTimeout(0) before the Material Icons Outlined font loaded. During that measurement, each icon <span> rendered its raw ligature text ('play_circle', 'edit_note', 'menu_book', …) in the fallback font, inflating each tab by ~90px. scrollWidth hit ~1600px, tripping the overflow branch and pushing 6 of 12 top-nav tabs into the '+' More popover at a 1440px viewport. Once detected, the hidden state persisted because no further re-measurement was triggered. Visually obvious on dashboard.html but affected any page on first-ever load. Fix (commits 35fdfdb0, 8e680d52): listen for document.fonts.ready to re-run updateOverflow once glyphs are available, AND reset moreBtn.style.display='none' at the top of updateOverflow so its 76px doesn't pre-consume tabWrap's flex width on the re-measurement.

Accessions table ID column had no max-width or word-break, so a single row with a 200+ char accession_id without break opportunities (e.g. 'X' repeated) expanded the column to thousands of pixels and pushed the other 8 columns off-screen for the ENTIRE table — Action buttons unreachable. Affected sort and filter views too.

confirmAdd interpolated raw accId into body markdown ('# ' + accId + ...). Markdown renderers pass inline HTML in headings/paragraphs through unchanged, so an accId containing <script>alert(...)</script> ended up as a real <script> element in the popup body DOM. Inert (innerHTML-set scripts don't execute, no XSS) but visually misleading — the H1 truncated at the < character because the rest was an invisible script element. Now HTML-escapes <, >, & before interpolation.

Open from cycle 20: lab-file-saved listener pushed a freshly-saved bottle as its own top-level inventory row instead of bumping the parent concept's bottle count. Reload fixed it. Now the listener detects type==='bottle' and full-reloads via loadInventory() so bottlesByConcept rebuilds correctly. Verified via dispatching a synthetic event and confirming no phantom bottle row appears.

bugs_found: WIKI-GRAPH-NOT-FILTERED (ux, fixed in cycle 25, commit 426be61e): wiki.html welcome-pane force-directed graph stayed full-color regardless of sidebar search / type-chip filter state. Fixed by adding matchSet to createForceGraph; non-matching nodes now dim to ~25% alpha and their edges fade. Carryover from cycle 19 (#48). Verified via verify02 screenshot (close-up of dimmed graph with 9 vibrant 'pistachio' matches) and computeWikiMatchSet runtime probe returning Set of 9 paths.

ID-COLUMN-WORD-BREAK (visual, fixed in cycle 26, commit 713b83bb): .acc-id-cell used overflow-wrap:anywhere from cycle 18 long-ID fix; this treated every hyphen/underscore as a soft break opportunity, so normal IDs like A1-T1-E stacked vertically into 3-4 lines because the column shrank to ~50px. Switched to overflow-wrap:break-word + min-width:96px so ordinary IDs stay on one line while truly unbreakable runs (200+ char force-typed IDs) still wrap.

PEOPLE-WIKILINK-SLASH-SPLIT (functional, fixed in cycle 26, commit 713b83bb): peopleCellHtml split the people field on /[,\/] which shredded slug-form wikilinks like [[people/grey-monroe]] into '[[people' + 'grey-monroe]]' — rendered as 6 plain-text pills instead of 3 clickable person pills. Extracted a splitPeople helper that ignores `,` and `/` inside [[...]] depth (bracket-counted), and reused it in populateFilters so the People filter dropdown also gets clean values.
CYCLE27-PRIORITY-NOT-STARS (visual, fixed in cycle 27, commit 230d4fde): accession popup Priority field rendered raw "2" instead of ★ glyphs
CYCLE27-PEOPLE-NOT-PILLS-IN-POPUP (visual, fixed in cycle 27, commit 230d4fde): accession popup People field rendered plain "A / B" text instead of individual pills
CYCLE27-INSTANCE-SUBTITLE-SLUG (visual, fixed in cycle 27, commit 230d4fde): renderBacklinksPane displayed parent slug "box dna extracts" instead of title "DNA Extracts Box"
CYCLE27-NOTEBOOK-RECENT-MN (cosmetic, closed in cycle 33 commit d59ed437; see CYCLE27-NOTEBOOK-RECENT-MN-V2): first recent notebook entry shows "MN" instead of YYYY-MM-DD date in subtitle
CYCLE27-OF-PLACEHOLDER-TRUNCATED (cosmetic, OPEN from cycle 27): New Tube of-field placeholder truncated to "or re..."

CYCLE27-DETAIL-LINK-NOT-CLICKABLE (ux, OPEN from cycle 27): accession popup Detail Sheet Link rendered as plain truncated text, not a clickable hyperlink [VERIFIED CLOSED in cycle 39: editor-modal.js:1565-1571 linkifies https field values; screenshot 09 confirms teal anchor + open_in_new icon. No new commit needed.]

bugs_found.title: 3 bottles point `of:` at deleted concept slugs
bugs_found.description: docs/stocks/bottle-test-item.md, bottle-test-item-mobile.md, bottle-ethyl-alcohol-absolute.md all referenced concept files that no longer exist (R21 dedup deleted ethyl-alcohol-absolute; the two test-items were stale fixtures with quantity 0).

Toast notifications fired from inside the editor modal (e.g. 'Location: <slug>' on parent picker pick, 'Saved' on em-save) appear at bottom:24px;right:24px and physically cover the green Save button. Toast z-index 99999 vs modal 10000.
bugs_found.fix_summary: Added body:has(.em-overlay.open) .toast-container { bottom: 96px; } in app/css/base.css — lifts toasts above the ~60px modal footer + 24px breathing room when the editor modal is open. Verified post-deploy in verify-toast-after-fix.png.

bugs_found.description: When opening object popup B immediately after saving object A (within ~3s), the breadcrumb pills above the popup body title are missing for B. The await Lab.editorModal.open() returns before parentChain async fully resolves on a freshly-invalidated hierarchy. Re-opening (or the after-save view) renders the breadcrumb fine. Bottle 1 popup (fresh page load) HAD breadcrumb; bottles 2 and 3 popups (opened post-save) did NOT. Suspected cause: hierarchy.invalidate() called inside patchObjectIndex during save clears the cached graph; the next openPopup races the rebuild and the await for parentChain returns empty silently due to the catch block at editor-modal.js ~line 968.
bugs_found.next_step: Either await window.Lab.hierarchy.build() explicitly inside openPopup before parentChain, or guard the breadcrumb branch with a small retry if chain length is 1. Suggested fix: change the breadcrumb branch in openPopup to await hierarchy.build() first.
bugs_found.closed_summary: Fixed in editor-modal.js openPopup breadcrumb branch by retrying parentChain after patchObjectIndex when chain<2 and meta.parent exists.

bugs_found.title: Calendar week header label off-by-2 (Mon–Fri instead of Mon–Sun)
Wikilink slugs with spaces (e.g. legacy [[Kehan Zhao]]) render as raw markdown text in Toast UI editor because the placeholder URL has an unescaped space
Wiki frontmatter chip URL rendered as plain text instead of clickable anchor (parallel to the cycle-27 popup bug)

bugs_found: CYCLE27-NOTEBOOK-RECENT-MN-V2 (cosmetic, fixed in cycle 33, commit d59ed437): cycle 32 extractDate fallback yielded the raw title "MN" for notebooks/vianney-ahn/fiberseq/MN.md. showWelcome in app/notebooks.html now awaits Lab.gh.fetchObjectIndex and uses the entry mtime (unix seconds) formatted YYYY-MM-DD when neither slug nor title yields a date. Path lookup tries "<path>.md" and "<path>". Verified deployed: subtitle now reads "2026-04-24".

bugs_found.summary: Wiki sidebar does not auto-expand folders during active search/type filter — matching docs hidden under collapsed categories
bugs_found.description: In renderNode the collapsed flag was based only on whether the folder contained the active doc. When a user searched the wiki sidebar for e.g. "ethanol" or "DNeasy", filterNode correctly pruned to matching folders, but every folder rendered collapsed by default, so the actual matching documents were not visible without an extra click on each category. Protocols and Notebooks both auto-expand on search; wiki was the outlier.
bugs_found.discovered_via: visual evaluation of screenshots 03-07 — sidebar showed only category headers after each search, no doc rows visible

bugs_found.summary: Dashboard Inventory Status alert list — status pill wraps to two lines for items with long names
bugs_found.description: loadInventoryStatus() renders each alert row as a flex container with three children: icon, title link, status pill. The pill <span> had no white-space:nowrap or flex-shrink:0, and the title <a> had no flex/min-width/text-overflow rules, so a long item title (e.g. 1-HYDROXY-3-(3-TRIFLUOROMETHYLPHENYL)UREA) wrapped to two lines and pushed the pill to wrap as well — the pill rendered as 'Needs' / 'More' across two lines.
bugs_found.discovered_via: visual evaluation of cycle36 screenshots 12 + 16 — pill on the 1-HYDROXY row clearly two-lined while shorter rows had a one-line pill

bugs_found.summary: Tapping the priority stars on a mobile accession card opens the editor modal instead of cycling priority — the click propagates to the parent .acc-card's onclick=openAccession.
bugs_found.description: renderTable() attaches the .pri-stars click listener with `tbody.querySelectorAll('.pri-stars').forEach(...)`, where `tbody` is the desktop table body. The mobile #cardView container also renders .pri-stars elements (in priorityStarsHtml), but they get no listener attached, so clicks on the stars walk up to the .acc-card wrapper which has its own onclick=openAccession. The result: on mobile, you can never bump priority via the stars — the modal opens instead. On desktop the table view stars work correctly because they are inside tbody.
bugs_found.discovered_via: cycle37/06-fatal-error.png — after a star tap on J4_T1_5 the editor modal opened with the card's full details instead of priority changing 3→0.

bugs_found.summary: Stats grid orphans 'Archived' tile alone in the last row on mobile when the Add Accession tile is visible (logged-in members) — 7 tiles in a 2-col grid is uneven.
bugs_found.description: The .acc-stats container has 7 stat-cards in source: addBtn (display:none until Lab.gh.isLoggedIn()), the Total counter, and 5 status counters (Active/Waiting/Storage/Completed/Archived). On mobile the @media (max-width: 768px) rule renders this as a 2-col grid. With auth, all 7 are visible and the last (Archived) sits alone on its own row, looking orphaned. Logged-out (6 visible) lays out cleanly as 3 rows of 2.
bugs_found.discovered_via: cycle37/01-accessions-mobile-baseline.png and 03-stats-grid-mobile.png — Archived clearly orphaned at bottom.

bugs_found.description: Editor modal popup view-mode status pill rendered in default grey #6b7280 for accession statuses (active/waiting/storage/completed/archived). The accessions tracker row pill rendered correct colors, so the same accession looked grey-pilled in the modal but amber/blue/etc on the row. Caused by app/js/editor-modal.js renderFields() statusColors+statusLabels maps only knowing the 7 inventory/waste statuses.

startEditing() in app/js/editor-modal.js (line 2676) called setupMobileToolbarToggle(editorEl, editor) with a bare 'editor' identifier that is not declared in the function's scope — should be currentEditor. ReferenceError fired immediately on every mobile-viewport Edit click, killing the FAB bar setup and leaving the body editor blank below the fields panel. Affected every object type opened in edit mode on a phone (accessions / notebooks / protocols / inventory / etc), not just accessions.

bugs_found.title: Tube popup FIELDS column truncates parent pill text
bugs_found.description: In the tube popup the FIELDS pane (left col, ~240px wide) truncates the Parent pill text mid-word (e.g. 'QA39 LibPrep Aliqu...'). title attribute provides the hover tooltip but the visual truncation is jarring. Consider letting the parent pill wrap, ellipsis-trimming at a word boundary, or shortening the field label so the value gets more room.
bugs_found.fix_attempt: cycle 40 commit 41605360 — switched pill text to white-space:normal + word-break:break-word in editor-modal.js upgradeParentField

bugs_found: CYCLE40-INVENTORY-QTY-DUPE (cosmetic, fixed in cycle 40, commit 41605360): inventory table rendered "2 bottles (2 bottles)" when bottle units heterogeneous; toInventoryRow falls back to count+"bottles" label, then qtyHtml suffix repeated it. Fix: suppress parenthetical when String(quantity)==String(containerCount) AND unit==instanceLabel.

TUBE-AS-CONTAINER (visual, fixed in cycle 41 — commit ce5dcb09): in the editor-modal backlinks pane, freshly-created tubes/bottles/etc. that weren't yet in object-index.json fell through to type='container' fallback, showing the box-cube icon and 'Container' subtitle. Fixed by inferring type from slug prefix (tube-, bottle-, sample-, extraction-, library-, pool-) and humanizing the slug title.

LABEL-MISMATCH (visual, fixed in cycle 41 — commit ce5dcb09): right column heading was hardcoded as 'Contents' for every popup, including concept popups (accessions, chemicals) where the pane actually shows backlinks. Now switches to 'References' with a link icon when the backlinks branch renders, resets to 'Contents' on every popup open.
fixes_applied: Live link-index patching for instant backlinks (app/js/github-api.js + app/js/editor-modal.js, commit 642579c)
Hierarchy cache invalidation on patchObjectIndex / removeFromObjectIndex (app/js/github-api.js, commit 3d94ff8)
Dashboard uses Lab.gh.fetchObjectIndex so the localStorage patch overlay flows through (app/dashboard.html, commit 763ad51)
`of` field renders a clickable object pill matching the `parent` field treatment — new data-of-pill placeholder + extended upgradeParentField (app/js/editor-modal.js, commit b0da836)
INDEX_KEYS now mirrors EXTRACT_KEYS — client-side localStorage overlay preserves waste-container + sample-tracker fields so freshly-saved rows render complete (app/js/github-api.js, commit 5c01bed)

fetchTree localStorage overlay (TREE_PATCH_KEY, 5-min TTL) — saveFile/deleteFile record path changes, fetchTree merges them so notebooks/wiki/protocols sidebars reflect just-committed files immediately (app/js/github-api.js, commit e0a0f44)

notebooks.html save/create/delete now call patchObjectIndex + patchLinkIndex (and removeFrom* on delete) so new notebook entries register as backlink sources on their wikilink targets without waiting for a deploy (app/notebooks.html, commit e0a0f44)

Breadcrumb separator-pill grouping in hierarchy.breadcrumbHTML — separators are now inside an inline-flex span together with the following pill so flex-wrap can't orphan a '/' at a wrapped line boundary (app/js/hierarchy.js, commit 25c4999)

Fields-column Of/Parent pill ellipsis — placeholder span gets min-width:0 + flex:1 + overflow:hidden; upgraded pill anchor gets max-width:100% + min-width:0 and wraps its title in a child span with overflow:hidden + text-overflow:ellipsis + white-space:nowrap; title="" attribute added for hover tooltip (app/js/editor-modal.js, commit 0088518)

calendar.html openAddEvent now calls gh.patchObjectIndex + gh.patchLinkIndex after gh.saveFile so freshly-created events survive a reload (and their member/notes wikilinks register as backlinks on their targets). hierarchy.js build() now skips parentResolved + children[] assignment for concept-type objects (reagent/chemical/buffer/solution/consumable/kit/enzyme/primer) so legacy LabBot fixtures with parent: no longer pollute container Contents or render a misleading physical breadcrumb (app/js/hierarchy.js, commit a92e314)

editor-modal.js: new member-field branch in renderFields emits data-member-pill placeholders for [[wikilinks]] in the value; upgradeParentField swaps them for real Person pills with a title-match fallback against the cached object index so display-name wikilinks like [[Dr. Monroe]] upgrade correctly (app/js/editor-modal.js, commit d8c96cc)

calendar.html: MEMBER_COLORS assignment now uses a djb2 hash of the member name modulo palette size instead of alphabetical index, so each member maps to a stable color and new members no longer reshuffle existing event colors (app/calendar.html, commit d8c96cc)

fixes_applied.what: Removed the dead #addSpecies input from the first form-row, leaving #addSpeciesField as the sole Species input. Reordered rows so Project + Species live on one row and People spans the next row alone.
setSort() now treats priority and mtime like last_updated — numeric/recency columns default to descending on first click so the highest-priority rows float to the top.
Refactored updateOverflow() to run the hide-trailing-tabs loop a second time after toggling the "+ More" button visible, so the shrunken tabWrap no longer leaves a partially-clipped trailing tab.

loadDoc() was already parsing frontmatter but only used `typeBadge + Lab.escHtml(docPath)` for the breadcrumb. Updated to prefer `meta.title` as the primary bold label and demote the slug to smaller secondary grey text (`breadcrumbLabel` ternary). Falls back to raw slug when frontmatter has no title.

fixes_applied: cycle 23: NAV-OVERFLOW-FONT-RACE (app/js/nav.js) — run updateOverflow again on document.fonts.ready and reset moreBtn display:none before measurement. commits 35fdfdb0 + 8e680d52.

cycle 24: ACC-LONG-TITLE-OVERFLOWS-TABLE (app/accessions.html) — added .acc-id-cell with max-width:360px + word-break:break-word + overflow-wrap:anywhere; renderTable now applies the class to the ID td.

cycle 24: ACC-MD-BODY-RAW-HTML (app/accessions.html confirmAdd) — HTML-escape <, >, & in accId before interpolating into auto-generated body markdown so a name with <script> renders as escaped text in the popup body H1, not as a real <script> element in DOM.

cycle 24: BOTTLE-NOT-ROLLED-UP-AFTER-CREATE (app/inventory.html) — lab-file-saved listener now full-reloads via loadInventory() when meta.type===bottle, so bottlesByConcept rebuilds correctly and the new bottle bumps the parent concept row instead of appearing as a phantom top-level row. commit ac58ff42 (carry-over swept from cycle 20).
Filter wiki welcome-pane graph to match sidebar search + type-chip state (matchSet, setMatchSet, computeWikiMatchSet, applyWikiGraphMatch). app/wiki.html commit 426be61e.

fixes_applied.description: In loadInventoryStatus(), added white-space:nowrap + flex-shrink:0 to the status pill <span> so the pill always renders on a single line, and added flex:1 + min-width:0 + overflow:hidden + text-overflow:ellipsis + white-space:nowrap + a title= tooltip to the title <a> so long names truncate cleanly with the full name available on hover.

fixes_applied.description: Changed the .pri-stars click handler attachment from `tbody.querySelectorAll('.pri-stars')` to `document.querySelectorAll('#tableBody .pri-stars, #cardView .pri-stars')` so both desktop and mobile views get the click listener with event.stopPropagation. Now tapping the stars on a mobile card cycles priority (0→1→2→3→0) instead of bubbling up to the parent .acc-card's openAccession handler.

fixes_applied.description: When Lab.gh.isLoggedIn() shows the Add Accession tile, also tag the .acc-stats container with class `has-add-tile`. Add a CSS rule in the @media (max-width: 768px) block: `.acc-stats.has-add-tile .stat-card:last-child { grid-column: 1 / -1; }` so the orphaned last tile (Archived) spans both columns and reads as a footer row instead. Logged-out users (6 cards = 3 even rows) are unaffected because the class is not added.
fixes_applied.verified: cycle37-verify/01-stats-grid-after-fix.png — Archived width = 343 (vs 167 before); occupies a full bottom row spanning both 2-col tracks. Mobile layout balanced.

Cycle 2: Alex Chen + cross_linker + Ethanol bottle day. Created bottle-ethanol-absolute-qa2-* pointing to the ethanol-absolute concept, placed on Chemistry Bench Area, then moved to Flammable Cabinet. All artifacts cleaned up. Hierarchy cache bug found and fixed (children + breadcrumbs now refresh immediately after save).

Cycle 3: Dr. Monroe + ui_critic + Reorder day. Toggled 3 items to needs_more, verified inventory stat cards and dashboard alert list. Caught a stale-dashboard-index bug (dashboard raw-fetched object-index.json, bypassing patch overlay). Fixed in commit 763ad51, verified via tests/qa-cycle-3-verify.mjs (kept as regression harness). All items restored to in_stock.

Cycle 4: Kayla Torres (undergrad) + cross_linker + Cross-page wikilink crawl. Read-only walk: Dashboard → QIAGEN DNeasy protocol → Ethanol Absolute reagent (popup) → ethanol-absolute bottle (popup with breadcrumb) → Flammable Cabinet (cabinet popup, 16 bottles) → Robbins Hall 0170 (room popup, 9 children). Found OF-FIELD-NOT-CLICKABLE bug: the `of` field on a bottle popup was plain text while `parent` was a clickable pill. Fix (commit b0da836) adds a parallel `data-of-pill` placeholder and extends upgradeParentField to handle both. Verified via tests/qa-cycle-4-verify.mjs — kept as regression harness.

Cycle 5: Kayla Torres (undergrad) + thorough + Waste container. Thorough-filled the waste.html Add Container form (long contents paragraph, Solid state, compound location). Found WASTE-FIELDS-NOT-IN-INDEX-KEYS bug: the table row showed blank Contents, blank State, '—' Days Held, and the state filter + content search excluded it because the client INDEX_KEYS whitelist dropped waste fields during patchObjectIndex. Fix (commit 5c01bed) syncs INDEX_KEYS with the server's EXTRACT_KEYS so contents/physical_state/started/etc. (plus sample-tracker fields) flow through the localStorage overlay. Verified via tests/qa-cycle-5-verify.mjs — both QA5 test files cleaned up via gh API.

Cycle 6: Vianney Ahn (grad student) + rushed + Lab notebook full day. Created a blank notebook entry with H1, two H2 sections, a 7-row NanoDrop results table, a callout, 4 wikilinks, a task list, and 2 small generated PNGs uploaded via Lab.editorModal.uploadImageBlob. Rushed double-saved. Reloaded; all rich elements re-rendered correctly including image data URLs via the GitHub-API live fallback. Found two bugs: FETCHTREE-NO-OVERLAY (sidebar folder count regresses after reload because GitHub's git/trees endpoint lags) and NOTEBOOKS-SAVE-NO-PATCH (notebooks.html bypasses patchObjectIndex/patchLinkIndex so backlink panels miss the new entry until next deploy). Both fixed in commit e0a0f44: fetchTree gets a TREE_PATCH_KEY localStorage overlay (5-min TTL) populated by saveFile/deleteFile, and notebooks.html save/create/delete now call patchObjectIndex+patchLinkIndex (or removeFrom*). Post-deploy verify (tests/qa-cycle-6-verify.mjs, kept as harness) confirmed fetchTree.sawIt=true and link-index hit count=1 for both wikilink targets. All cycle-6 test artifacts (4 notebook entries + 4 uploaded images + 1 verify entry) cleaned up via gh API.

Cycle 7: Alex Chen (postdoc) + ui_critic + New freezer box day. Created a new 10x10 box 'Library Prep Kit Storage' on shelf-minus80-a-1 plus 3 tubes at A1 / C5 / J10 to stress grid rendering at top-left, middle, and bottom-right corners. Verified the shelf now lists the box as a child, the empty grid renders 100 cells, the filled grid correctly places the 3 tubes with teal fill + label fragments, and each tube's 5-level breadcrumb renders with all correct type-colored pills. Found and fixed the cycle-1-logged BREADCRUMB-DANGLING-SLASH cosmetic bug: hierarchy.breadcrumbHTML was rendering each separator as an independent flex-wrap child, so a bare '/' could end up orphaned at a wrapped line boundary. Fix groups separator+following-pill inside an inline-flex span so wrap can only break between groups (commit 25c4999). Verified post-deploy at 3 viewport widths (1440/900/520) via tests/qa-cycle-7-verify.mjs — endsWithOrphanSlash:false for every line. All cycle-7 test artifacts cleaned up via gh API. Also noted a minor new issue (DNA-QUANTIFICATION-PILL-UNTYPED) for follow-up. NAV-TRUNCATION is no longer reproducible at 1440 — the 'Lab Map' tab now fits fully; only a far-right tab gets clipped.

Cycle 8: Vianney Ahn (grad student) + cross_linker + Concept+instance linking. Picked a reagent concept with 0 existing physical bottles (LabBot Freezer Item mnthp11v — one of 15 zero-instance candidates out of ~170 reagents; 156 already had bottles via `of:`). Created 2 bottles with distinct lots/expirations/quantities, both placed on cabinet-chemical. Verified: concept CONTENTS column now shows 2 INSTANCES IN LAB immediately with per-bottle subtitles (500 mL/250 mL · cabinet-chemical); inventory collapses them under a single concept row with '750 mL (2 bottles)' aggregate badge; 3-level bottle breadcrumb renders cleanly; clickable Of pill round-trips to concept; clickable Parent pill opens cabinet popup; cabinet's children count bumps 107→109; lab-map 'QA8' filter shows both bottles under Chemical Cabinet. Found OF-PILL-TRUNCATED-NO-ELLIPSIS: Of/Parent pills in the 300px fields column clipped long titles mid-word with no ellipsis because the pill anchor had white-space:nowrap and no max-width, and wrapping span lacked min-width:0+flex:1. Fix (commit 0088518) constrains the placeholder with min-width:0+flex:1+overflow:hidden, sets max-width:100%+min-width:0 on the upgraded anchor, and wraps the title in a child span with text-overflow:ellipsis; icon stays visible; title="" attribute for hover tooltip. Post-deploy verify at 1440 and 520 widths via tests/qa-cycle-8-verify.mjs — pillInsideCol:true, textEllipsis:true, titleAttr='LabBot Freezer Item mnthp11v'. Also surfaced CONCEPT-HAS-PARENT-BREADCRUMB as a data-quality issue (LabBot test artifacts in production have `parent:` set on concept frontmatter, placing them as physical-location children — left open). All cycle-8 test artifacts cleaned up via gh API.

Cycle 10: Alex Chen (postdoc) + ui_critic + Equipment move day. Created 3 QA test bottles (qa10movepecs-a/b/c, each pointing to resources/ethanol-absolute via `of:`) under cabinet-flammable, then used the editor's locations picker to move all 3 to cabinet-corrosive. Verified: (a) each bottle's own popup breadcrumb and parent pill updated live to the new chain (no redeploy), (b) flammable cabinet Contents list stayed at 16 baseline (bottles pruned), (c) corrosive cabinet Contents went 15 → 18, (d) lab-map tree filter 'qa10movepecs' shows all 3 grouped under Corrosive Cabinet, (e) inventory search shows the new location in the LOCATION column. Swept 3 carry-over cycle-9 cosmetic/ux bugs in the same cycle: (1) TOPNAV-WIKI-CLIPPED CLOSED — tightened desktop tab padding to 12px 8px with 4px gap so the full 'Wiki' label fits at 1440px (mirrored trim below 1280px); (2) MEMBER-FIELD-RAW-WIKILINK CLOSED — added a member-field branch to renderFields in editor-modal.js that emits data-member-pill placeholders, upgradeParentField swaps them for real Person pills with a title-match fallback for display-name wikilinks like [[Dr. Monroe]]; (3) MEMBER-COLOR-SHUFFLE CLOSED — replaced alphabetical-index MEMBER_COLORS lookup with djb2 hash modulo palette size so each member maps to the same color regardless of schedule additions. All fixes in commit d8c96cc. Verified post-deploy via cycle10-verify screenshots: full Wiki label at 1440, event popup shows Grey Monroe as a clickable Person pill, existing barb-m events stay green after adding a new Dr. Monroe event. All cycle-10 test artifacts (3 bottles + 2 verify events) cleaned up via gh API DELETE before commit.

Cycle 15: Alex Chen (postdoc) + impatient + Rename + relink day. Fresh combination (impatient modifier never used before, scenario never logged — earlier cycle-13 attempt rolled up into Backlinks sanity instead). Flow: create disposable protocol qa15-initial-proto-* in wet-lab with type:protocol frontmatter, then a notebook entry qa15-rename-consumer-* under test-user/ that wikilinks to it both as bare [[slug]] and as path-qualified [[wet-lab/slug]]. Baseline pre-rename: both pills render as purple Protocol pills with book icon (screenshot 03). Rename: stub Lab.modal.prompt to return NEW_TITLE, then DOUBLE-CLICK the Rename button. First click succeeds: new file created, old file deleted, NAV updated, breadcrumb + H1 reflect new title, Wet Lab Basics sidebar count bumps 45→46, history.pushState to new URL, success toast fires. Second click races: it enters renameDoc synchronously before the first await, both pass through renameInFlight check (unset at the time), and the second's fetchFile on the stale path 404s → red 'Rename failed: Not Found' toast. Classic impatient-modifier race. IMPATIENT-RENAME bug logged + fixed in-cycle via renameInFlight guard set BEFORE the first await (commit c3fb6b6). Then exercised old-slug deep-link behavior (DOUBLE-LOAD-ERROR-PREFIX — 'Failed to load: Failed to load docs/.../foo.md (HTTP 404)' on protocols/wiki/projects/notebooks) — fixed with a shared Lab.formatLoadError helper that maps 404s to 'Document not found. It may have been renamed or deleted.' and strips the redundant prefix. Also shortened 'Search protocols...' placeholder to 'Search...' to stop the trailing-s clipping in the narrow protocols sidebar input. Post-rename reload of the notebook entry confirmed updateWikilinksAfterRename (QA13 plumbing, landed in a prior run) correctly rewrote BOTH bare [[slug]] and path-qualified [[dir/slug]] wikilinks to the new slug — hasOldSlug=false, hasNewSlug=true in the live markdown, and both pills render as purple Protocol pills with icon and navigate to the renamed protocol on click. Verify run (qa-cycle-15-verify.mjs): triple-clicked Rename → rename_failed_toast_present:false; nonexistent-slug deep-links on protocols + wiki + notebooks all show 'Document not found. It may have been renamed or deleted.'; placeholder 'Search...' renders complete with no clipping. All 3 test files cleaned up via gh API DELETE.

Cycle 9: Dr. Monroe (PI) + careful + Calendar add/delete. First exercise of the calendar route across all 9 cycles. Full flow: week nav (prev/next/this-week), click-to-create at Wed 14:00 (modal prefilled from cell coords), fill title + [[Dr. Monroe]] member + notes with [[qiagen-dneasy-extraction]] wikilink, submit, event lands in correct grid cell with green Dr. Monroe pill, GH API confirms file save, click block opens editor-modal popup (type=event pill, fields, body w/ clickable QIAGEN DNeasy pill, contents), close via Escape, filter-by-Dr.-Monroe shows only our event, reload. FUNCTIONAL BUG FOUND: CALENDAR-NO-INDEX-PATCH — the event disappears after reload because openAddEvent didn't call patchObjectIndex/patchLinkIndex (same pattern as cycle-6 NOTEBOOKS-SAVE-NO-PATCH). Fix (commit 8a679ba): after gh.saveFile, call gh.patchObjectIndex with all event frontmatter + gh.patchLinkIndex against member+notes for wikilinks. Also adds 'recurrence_id' to INDEX_KEYS to mirror EXTRACT_KEYS (cycle-5 pattern). Verified via tests/qa-cycle-9-verify.mjs — afterReload.landed=true with correct day/hour, localStorage overlay keys present, link targets=['Dr. Monroe', 'qiagen-dneasy-extraction']. Also exercised: delete via Lab.gh.deleteFile (works, persists), mobile 375px (day-tab row + single-column grid), weekly recurring series (4 occurrences Apr 21→May 12 all sharing recurrence_id, navigation shows occurrence next week). Swept 2 carried-over items: (1) CONCEPT-HAS-PARENT-BREADCRUMB CLOSED via app/js/hierarchy.js CONCEPT_TYPES guard (skip parent handling for reagent/chemical/buffer/solution/consumable/kit/enzyme/primer) commit a92e314 + deletion of the 2 remaining stale LabBot fixtures (labbot-freezer-item-mnthp11v, tube-item) commit f7cfe1f; (2) DNA-QUANTIFICATION-PILL-UNTYPED CLOSED as not-a-bug after investigation (the slug was a cycle-7 test typo; an unresolved slug correctly renders as a plain teal pill). Logged 3 new open cosmetic/ux items: TOPNAV-WIKI-CLIPPED (Wiki tab clips to 'Wik' at 1440), MEMBER-FIELD-RAW-WIKILINK (event popup member field not pill-upgraded like parent/of), MEMBER-COLOR-SHUFFLE (alphabetical index causes existing events to recolor when a new member joins). All cycle-9 test event files (1 single + 4 recurring + 1 verify) cleaned up via gh API.

Cycle 19: Kayla Torres (undergrad) + confused + Search every surface. Fresh combo — confused modifier never used before, scenario never tested. Kayla searches on 5 surfaces (inventory/protocols/notebooks/wiki/waste) with a real query, a typo, weird input (emoji + em-dash + <script> XSS), and empty. Finding: XSS is properly escaped (invXssAlert=false), all 5 per-page searches work functionally, but protocols + notebooks had minimal plain-text 0-result empty states while inventory/waste used the styled .empty-state with search_off icon — fixed in this cycle. Also confirmed: dashboard has no global search (no cross-surface search exists); wiki sidebar search filters the folder tree but leaves the knowledge graph in the main pane completely unfiltered even at 0 matches (logged WIKI-GRAPH-NOT-FILTERED as OPEN for a future cycle). Swept 2 cycle-18 carry-over items to closed: (1) FAB-NEAR-WIZARD-NEXT-MOBILE — actually OVERLAPS Next button by ~34px horizontally, not just 'near' as cycle 18 described. Fixed with body.wizard-open class toggle + CSS rule hiding #issue-reporter-btn. (2) NOTEBOOKS-PERSON-PARAM-IGNORED — fixed by reading ?person=<slug> on notebooks init and pre-filling #nbSearch with the prettified slug, so the sidebar filters to just that person's folder. Onboarding wizard's success checklist 'Write your first lab notebook entry' link now correctly lands new users on their own folder. All fixes verified live post-deploy via tests/qa-cycle-19-verify.mjs — FAB display=none during wizard, personParam showsOtherFolders=false, both empty states iconText='search_off'. 28 screenshots captured in cycle19/, 5 verify screenshots. No test artifacts created on GitHub, nothing to clean up.

Cycle 18: Alex Chen (postdoc) + thorough + New user onboarding. First exercise of /app/people.html onboarding wizard end-to-end and first fresh test of the People + Profile routes (both added to routes_tested). Wizard creates: person md + notebook folder + first notebook entry across 3 GitHub API writes. Found 3 bugs — all fixed in commit 52fcfd3: (1) PEOPLE-CARD-PILLS-BARE-SLUG (functional, widespread) — every connected pill on every person card used ?doc=<bare-slug>, 404ing for any protocol/project living in a subfolder like wet-lab/autoclave; fix resolves the slug against the cached object-index and uses obj.path (minus .md, minus /index for folder-style pages). (2) PERSON-DASHBOARD-IGNORES-FORWARD-LINKS (functional/ux) — the person dashboard aggregated inbound backlinks only, so a freshly-onboarded person landed on a 0-across-the-board dashboard despite their body having [[protocol]]/[[project]] wikilinks from the wizard; fix merges outbound edges from the link-index plus a body-parse fallback into backlinkedItems. (3) NOTEBOOK-ENTRY-NOT-PATCHED (functional) — the wizard patched only the person card into the object-index overlay, not the first notebook entry, so the Notebooks filter (path startsWith notebooks/<slug>/) read 0 until the next deploy; fix also patches the entry path with {type:'notebook', title}. Verified live via tests/qa-cycle-18-verify.mjs: autoclave pill click lands on the real protocol page (no 'Document not found'); fresh-user dashboard shows 1 Notebook / 2 Protocols / 1 Project; sections expand to show the right rows. Logged 2 follow-up items as OPEN: NOTEBOOKS-PERSON-PARAM-IGNORED (wizard links to notebooks.html?person=<slug> but notebooks.html doesn't filter by it) and FAB-NEAR-WIZARD-NEXT-MOBILE (issue-reporter FAB crowds the wizard Next button at 375px). All cycle-18 test artifacts (cycle person + entry + gitkeep, verify person + entry + gitkeep) cleaned up via gh API DELETE.
